## Guidelines

This manual contains notes of varying importance that should be read with care:

- **Important**: Highlights key information on handling the product, the product itself, or a particular part of the documentation.
- **Note**: Provides supplementary information regarding handling the product, the product itself, or a specific part of the documentation.

### Disclaimer of Liability

We have reviewed the contents of this publication to ensure consistency with the hardware and software described. Since variance cannot be precluded entirely, we cannot guarantee full consistency. However, the information in this publication is reviewed regularly and any necessary corrections are included in subsequent editions.

### Cybersecurity Information

Siemens provides products and solutions with industrial cybersecurity functions that support the secure operation of plants, systems, machines and networks. In order to protect plants, systems, machines and networks against cyber threats, it is necessary to implement and continuously maintain a holistic, state-of-the-art industrial cybersecurity concept. Siemens products and solutions constitute one element of such a concept. Customers are responsible for preventing unauthorized access to their plants, systems, machines and networks. Such systems, machines and components should only be connected to an enterprise network or the internet if and to the extent such a connection is necessary and only when appropriate security measures (for example, firewalls and/or network segmentation) are in place. For additional information on industrial cybersecurity measures that may be implemented, please visit https://www.siemens.com/cybersecurity-industry.

Siemens products and solutions undergo continuous development to make them more secure. Siemens strongly recommends that product updates are applied as soon as they are available and that the latest product versions are used. Use of product versions that are no longer supported, and failure to apply the latest updates may increase the customer's exposure to cyber threats. To stay informed about product updates, subscribe to the Siemens Industrial Cybersecurity RSS feed at https://www.siemens.com/cert.

## 1 Basic Know-How Before Getting Started

Before you start using the Solutions released by Opcenter Execution Discrete, here are the essentials to familiarizing
with the application, performing basic operations and taking advantage of certain Apps.
For details regarding the prerequisites and the Workflow to follow, see Quick Start to Using Opcenter Execution
Discrete.
This Manual provides detailed information on how to work within the UI Applications installed with the
product without specifying which App or Extension App provides each functionality. The same procedures
can be used as a reference also for custom solutions, provided that their UI Applications include the UI
modules and screens of the Apps or Extension Apps related to each functionality.

The operations to be performed in the UI Applications provided by the available Solutions are described in the
current manual.

Accessing the Applications
1. Access the login page clicking the Opcenter Execution Discrete shortcut.
The direct paths of the Applications are:
http://<hostname>/sit-ui/runtime/U4DM.Siemens_SIT_UADM
http://<hostname>/sit-ui/runtime/DS4AM.Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM
http://<hostname>/sit-ui/runtime/DSBasic.Siemens_OPC_EXDSBasic
http://<hostname>/sit-ui/runtime/DSAdvanced.Siemens_OPC_EXDSAdvanced.
2. Log in to the Opcenter EX DS web page in either of the following ways:
Insert the credentials of a valid user and click Sign In.
Click Use your current Windows session to Log In to access the application with the current user
credentials.

The Home Page
The Home page represents your starting point for familiarizing with the product. It contains all available tiles for
accessing the numerous pages containing the available functionalities. To facilitate navigation within the Home
Page, these tiles have been grouped on the basis of the type of functionalities they offer:
Production Coordination: tiles for assisting Production Coordinators in monitoring production inside their
plant and work in progress, as well as for verifying data acquired during production.
Shopfloor Execution: tiles for assisting Operators in their tasks on the shopfloor during execution in regard to
work order progress and history, logistics, additive manufacturing, etc.
Product and Production Configuration: tiles for creating and configuring entities representing the essential
engineering elements that come into play to execute production.
System Configuration: tiles related to basic functionalities as configuration keys, log management or template
configuration.
The Home Page comes provided with four dedicated navigation cards representing the aforementioned tile groups:
clicking the card of your choice allows you to jump directly to the tile group containing the tile you require, and
then clicking each tile allows you to jump to the related page. Unlike the home page, all other pages contain a
sidebar displaying icons that correspond to the aforementioned tile groups, thus allowing you to easily access each
page without returning to the home page. All procedures contained in this manual describe how to access the UI
Application pages from the sidebar, but you can also start from the home page searching for the related tile name.
If desired, this standard layout can be modified according to your needs: for example, it is possible to
create custom navigation cards or move tiles from one category to another of your preference. For
information on these customizations, see Configuring the UI Application Menu in the Opcenter Execution
Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.
In addition, to facilitate navigation, you can focus solely on certain tiles that satisfy your needs by using the Search
filter provided at the top of the Home page: if you enter a text in the Search filter, only those tiles containing it in
their label will be displayed, according to the tile group to which they belong.

Selecting and searching for elements
All pages come provided with both a Smart Filter and a dynamic Search. Fields that accept elements of a list as
input offer both a dynamic filter and a Browse

button, which opens a dedicated search window.

On pages that display elements in tile format, you can access the element details in the following ways:
selecting the tile and clicking
clicking

Open.

on the upper-right corner of the tile.

Applying User Preferences
User Preferences can be used to customize the look and feel of your UI in intuitive ways. You can customize the
current view by setting User Preferences. Click the
button to choose the column visibility, column order, and
tile property visibility. You can specify the properties you want to show, and their sequence. For more information,
see Setting User Preferences in the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide, and
Opcenter Execution Foundation Apps Overview in the Opcenter Execution Foundation User Manual.

Import and Export of Data from the Runtime Database
If the system is properly configured, in several screens you can click
Export to download selected entities and
instances from the database. The data can be subsequently modified and eventually imported on the same or

another production machine. For details, refer to the How To Export and Import Data section in the Opcenter
Execution Foundation User Manual.

### 1.1 Apps and Extension Apps included in the Available Discrete Solutions

The Opcenter Execution Discrete Solutions include the installation of a set of Apps and Extension Apps either
specific to Discrete manufacturing or provided by the underlying platform. The following pages list them providing
an overview of the related functionalities:
Opcenter Execution DiscreteApps
Opcenter Execution Discrete Consolidated Extended Model Apps
Opcenter Execution Discrete Extension Apps
Opcenter Execution Foundation Apps
Opcenter Execution Foundation Extension Apps

Low Code UI Apps
In addition to the functional Apps and Extension Apps listed above, the product releases a set of Low Code UI
Applications powered by Mendix available for specific needs pertaining to different types of production:

Non-Conformances

The screens available in the Opcenter EX DS Product Engineer and Opcenter EX DS Production Coordinator
Apps require that the Legacy Apps be installed correctly in order to function.

Focus inside Tabs
In all UI pages of the engineering Low Code UI Apps, if you are operating in the Overview tab, the buttons found on
the right-hand toolbar will focus on the main entity. Instead, if you are working inside any other tab, a horizontal
toolbar will allow you to work on the content currently displayed inside the tab, whereas, if you click on the buttons
found on the right-hand toolbar, the focus will remain on the main entity.

Alternative navigation to Elements
If you are using Low Code UI Apps to configure your production, you will notice a different user experience,
compared to the legacy screens.
You will no longer find the left column populated with the list of elements to be selected to navigate to their details.
Instead, to navigate to the details of a different element, you can select the last breadcrumb element that appears
as a link and switch to another element from the drop-drown list. This solution improves the responsiveness of the
application and renders the details content in fullwidth mode.
The transition to this alternative navigation is in progress so it is not yet available for all screens.

Display of Archived Data
If you have configured Archiving and you are using Low Code UI Apps to manage your production, depending on the
configured Maintenance Archiving rule, you will be able to display archived data only, by selecting the Show
Archived Data toggle where visible. In this modality, any other functionality, for example the creation of new Work
Orders, will not work, even if the buttons are visible.
In case of errors in displaying data, verify that:
you have run the scripts also on the Archiving Database, while deploying Low Code UI Apps, as explained in
section Deploying Low Code UI Apps.
you have properly configured the Maintenance Archiving rules. as explained in section How to Configure
Archiving of the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.

### 1.2 Speeding Up Operator Landing Page Loading through Task Customization

Opcenter Execution Discrete comes provided with a pre-defined set of Tasks related to material consumption, Print
Job File management, quality inspection, etc.. This pre-defined set of Tasks forms a Process Definition which is
released by default by Opcenter Execution Foundation and is known as UADMProcessDefinition. When operating
in Runtime, all of the Tasks provided by UADMProcessDefinition are available for use, as they are all loaded into
your system.
Given that your system might not need all of the Tasks available (for example, if you are not working in an Additive
Manufacturing context, you will not require the Task for Print Job File management), you can tailor the contents of
the default UADMProcessDefinition to your needs. By limiting the content of your default UADMProcessDefinition
to one or a sub-set of the available Tasks offered by default, the Operator Landing Page is loaded more quickly.
For more information, see How to Manage Process Definitions in the Opcenter Execution Foundation User Manual.
Remember that Process Definitions work in synergy with what are known as Operation/Step Categories,
which require appropriate configuration in order to function properly: for more information, see How to
Configure Operation/Step Categories.

Target User
Product Engineer

Procedure
1. Operating in the Home page, click the

Process Definitions tile.

2. Select UADMProcessDefinition and then click
Copy Revision in the sidebar on the right: this opens the
Copy Revision dialog.
3. Enter values for Id and New Revision and click Save: the copy of the Revision is now listed as a Process
Definition in the Process Definitions page.
4. Select the Process Definition you have just made.
5. Click
Open the flow editor for the selected process definition in the sidebar on the right: the flow editor
opens, displaying the complete set of Tasks making up the original default UADMProcessDefinition.
6. Operating in the flow editor, click each Task that you want to eliminate: a broken-line border is displayed
surrounding the Task.
7. On the broken-line border, click the
Delete icon on the horizontal icon bar at the top of the flow editor: the
selected Task is removed.
8. Repeat steps 6 and 7 for each of the remaining Tasks that you want to remove.
You can leave a single Task, or a combination of Tasks inside the Process Definition you are editing,
according to your needs.
9. Click
Save in the sidebar on the right: the selected Process Definition now contains solely those Tasks of
your preference.
10. Click

Set Current in the sidebar on the right and then click Yes.

11. Click

Set Executable in the sidebar on the right and then click Yes.

Speeding Up Operator Landing Page Loading through Task Customization

## 2 Quick Start to Using Opcenter Execution Discrete

Here is a general outline of the main macro-steps that must be executed in order to use Opcenter Execution
Discrete properly and to full advantage.
Keep in mind that:
Not all users can perform all the operations cited: before attempting to perform the operation(s) making up a
macro-step, check that you possess the appropriate user rights to do so.
Depending on your Solution, not all the operations cited are available in the corresponding UI Application.
Integration with Other Systems
If you intend to use Opcenter EX DS in integration contexts, before proceeding with the Workflow below,
see How to Integrate Opcenter Execution Discrete with Other Systems for dedicated information.

Prerequisites
The shortcut containing the URL to the Opcenter EX DS web user interface has been copied from the desktop of
the production machine (where the shortcut has been created by the Opcenter EX DS configuration tool)
to the desktop of the web client machine. The URL in the shortcut's properties has been modified, changing the
machine name accordingly.
Note SSL connections are also supported.
The hosts file, available in %windir%\System32\drivers\etc, maps the production machine hostname to the IP
address.

Workflow
1. Configure the production environment.
2. Create and prepare Human Resources and their capabilities.
3. Configure the production processes.
4. Prepare production execution.
5. Depending on the type of Runtime working environment involved, perform one of the following:
Execute Work Orders in the Operator Landing Page (legacy)
Execute Work Orders in the Operator Terminal for Complex Manufacturing
Execute Work Orders in the Operator Terminal for Repetitive Manufacturing
Execute Inspection Orders in the Operator Terminal for Quality Execution
Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow.
6. (Optional) Execute production on Execution Groups.
7. (Optional) Manage Inspection Orders.
8. (Optional) Manage Non-Conformances.
9. (Optional) Manage Change Packages.
10. Monitor (and, if necessary, tweak) production execution.

Additional Operations
Execute production in Offline mode.
Manage Additive Manufacturing-related Activities.
Manage Buffer replenishment and transport operations.
Handle Notifications.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Speeding Up Operator Landing Page Loading through Task Customization

3 How to Configure the Production Environment
Before configuring the production processes you want to manage, you must properly configure the system, defining
the structure of the plant in which the production processes will take place, and then configuring a set of basic
entities that will be used to model the production activities.
The workflow to be followed depends on your specific needs. According to the type of production you intend to use,
configure the entities that are relevant to your production process. Keep also in mind that not all general
configurations mentioned at step 1 may be relevant for the type of production you are going to use.

Main Workflow
1. Perform General Configurations.
2. Define the Plant Structure.
3. Configure Numbering Patterns.
4. (for Production Flow Control Diagrams) Configure Line Sequences.
5. Configure Barcode Rules.
6. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Final Product Types.
7. Configure Reasons.
8. (optional) Configure Documents.
9. Configure Failures.
10. Configure Tool Definitions.
11. (for Screwing Operations) Configure Screwing Tool Definitions.
12. (for Open Protocol Node Operations) Configure Open Protocol Tool Definitions.
13. Configure Materials.
14. (for Additive Manufacturing) Configure Powder Materials.
15. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Bills of Materials for Product Configurations.
16. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Bills of Features.
17. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Product Configurations.
18. (for Additive Manufacturing) Configure Substrates.
19. (optional) Configure Custom Logistic Classes.
20. Configure Buffer Definitions.
21. Configure Container Types.
22. Configure Operation/Step Categories.
23. Manage Catalogs.
24. (for Additive Manufacturing) Configure Plugins.
25. (for Additive Manufacturing) Configure 3D Printers.
26. (for Additive Manufacturing) Configure Print Job Files.
27. Configure Rework Codes.
28. Configure Non-Productive Activities.
29. (for DNC Integration) Import and Map CNC Packages.
30. Configure Work Operations.
31. Configure Custom Non-Conformance Lifecycles.
32. Configure Custom Interlocking Checks.
33. (optional) Configure Custom Result Types.
34. Link Items to Buffer Definitions.
35. Configure Setpoints.
36. Configure Line Side Positions.
37. Configure Qualification Criteria.
38. Configure Output Messages.
39. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Production Flow Control Diagrams.
40. (for One Piece Flow production) Configure Quality Gates.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

### 3.1 Applying General Configuration Settings

The system provides a dedicated page for accessing the general parameters that you must set to pre-configure the
system before starting to work with it.
The parameters are grouped by logical functionality.

Target User
Users with the Product Engineer role can perform this action.

Procedure
1. Click
System Configuration > Configuration Keys in the sidebar.
2. Select one of the following tabs and set the relative parameters according to your needs:
Engineering
Runtime
Results
Additive Manufacturing
Integrations
E-Mail
CLM Integration
AMN Integration
Certifications
OPC IN Integration
3. Click Save.

#### 3.1.1 Engineering Configuration Settings

The following General Configuration Settings are related to Engineering functionalities.
Configuration Keys in Complex and Repetitive Manufacturing Contexts
In this release, only the following Engineering configuration keys affect Complex and Repetitive
Manufacturing contexts:
Save Quick Search
Non-Conformance Lifecycle for Work Order.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Propagate
Segregation
Tags Behavior

Manages the propagation of the Data Segregation tags from the Final Material to the As
Planned BoP and the related link to Documents and Work Instructions.
Possible values:
ADD: default value. The propagation of the segregation tag attached to the Final Material
is added to the already existing tags of the As Planned BoP and related link to
Documents and Work Instructions.
REPLACE: The propagation of the segregation tag attached to the Final Material replaces
any already existing tags of the As Planned BoP and related link to Documents and Work
Instructions.

Enable
automatic
Work Order
Header merge

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
Defines how the system must manage the Work Order Headers.
Possible values:
NO: Work Order Headers are not automatically merged with As Planned BOPs (default
behavior).
DEFAULT: a Work Order Header is automatically merged with a downloaded As Planned
BOP. The generated Work Order is then released for production according to the setting
of the Enable automatic Work Order Header release configuration key.
CUSTOM: Reserved for custom project implementations.

Enable
automatic
Work Order
Header release

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
Defines how the system must manage the release of Work Orders generated from Work
Order Headers that have been automatically merged with downloaded As Planned BOPs.
Possible values:
NO: Work Orders are not automatically released for production.
DEFAULT: Work Orders are automatically released for production (default behavior).
CUSTOM: Reserved for custom project implementations.

NonConformance
Lifecycle for
Machine

Identifier of the Lifecycle used for Quality Non-Conformances associated to Machines.
Recommended value: QUALITY ITEM WORKFLOW, that is associated to Machines, Tools and
Material Tracking Units/Containers.
If you configured a custom Non-Conformance Lifecycle, you can insert its identifier here.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

NonConformance
Lifecycle for
Material
Tracking Unit

Identifier of the Lifecycle used for Quality Non-Conformances associated to Material
Tracking Units.
Recommended value: QUALITY ITEM WORKFLOW, that is associated to Machines, Tools and
Material Tracking Units/Containers.
If you configured a custom Non-Conformance Lifecycle, you can insert its identifier here.
If you want to use Failures in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Manufacturing, select DefectWF.

NonConformance
Lifecycle for
Tool

Identifier of the Lifecycle used for Quality Non-Conformances associated to Tools.
Recommended value: QUALITY ITEM WORKFLOW, that is associated to Machines, Tools and
Material Tracking Units/Containers.
If you configured a custom Non-Conformance Lifecycle, you can insert its identifier here.

NonConformance
Lifecycle for
Work Order

Identifier of the Lifecycle used for Quality Non-Conformances associated to Work Order
Operations.
Recommended Value: QUALITY WORKFLOW, that is associated to Work Order Operations.
If you configured a custom Non-Conformance Lifecycle, you can insert its identifier here.

NonConformance
on Defect or
Failure

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
Used to change the behavior of the system when creating Non-Conformances on Machines,
Material Tracking Units, Tools and Work Order Operations.
Defect: You will be prompted to select Defects and Defect Groups.
Failure: (default) You will be prompted to select custom Failures and Sub-Failures. Note:
This value is recommended if you want to use the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Manufacturing.

Save Quick
Search

If the checkbox is selected, the system saves the last search criteria used and proposes it to
the user when a new search is performed. This is applicable only for Operator Landing pages.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key
Use Latest
Version of As
Planned BOP

Description

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
(Used in case of integration with Teamcenter Manufacturing) Defines how the system must
behave if some changes have been made on the As Planned BOP in terms of Material, Tool
Definition, Operations or Steps.
Possible values:
NO: Out-Of-Date As Planned BOPs can be used to generate Work Orders (default
behavior).
DEFAULT: The system verifies if an out-of-date version of the As Planned BOP is available
in Opcenter Execution Discrete and, in this case, requests that the entire structure be
downloaded from Teamcenter Manufacturing.
CUSTOM: Reserved for custom project implementations.

Pre-kit
Validation
Default

This checkbox is displayed as selected by default: in this case, when you attempt to execute
pre-kitting on Materials for which no Serial Number or Batch has already been selected, the
Validate checkbox in the Pre-Kit Serial Numbers/Batches panel will be set to True by
default, thereby bypassing manual setting of this value for each Serial Number or Batch to
be pre-kitted. If you do not want validation on all of these Serial Numbers or Batches, you
can disable this configuration key: for single instances in which you do not want want
validation, unselect the Validate checkbox manually.

Containment
Request
Lifecycle

Identifier of the Lifecycle used to manage Containment Requests.
Default value: ContainmentRequestLifecycle.
If you configured a custom Lifecycle, you can insert its identifier here.

#### 3.1.2 Runtime Configuration Settings

The following General Configuration Settings are related to Runtime functionalities.
Configuration Keys in Complex and Repetitive Manufacturing Contexts
In this release, only the following Runtime configuration keys affect Complex and Repetitive
Manufacturing contexts:
Show only Work Order Operation ready to be started
Runtime Failure Association Management
Allow Opening a Non-Conformance on Parent Failure
Auto-Consume Batch Materials
Pause/Complete Work Order Operation by Different User
Check Material Consumption.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Allow Opening a
NonConformance on
Parent Failure

This configuration key is used to decide whether it is possible to open a Non-Conformance
using Failures that are not leaves of the Failure's hierarchy or not.
Possible values:
TRUE: If this option is selected, then any Failure at any level of the Failure's hierarchy
tree can be used.
FALSE: If this option is selected, then only the leaves of the Failure's hierarchy tree can
be used i.e. a Failure with Sub-Failures cannot be used to open a Non-Conformance.

Allow more
Material
Consumption on
Batch WO

This configuration key affects only Material Consumption command behavior for
TransferBatch and FlexibleBatch Work Orders.
By default, this configuration key is set to True, thus permitting Material Consumptions that
exceed the threshold set for a TransferBatch or FlexibleBatch Work Order.
By deselecting this checkbox, any attempt to perform a similar Material Consumption on a
TransferBatch or FlexibleBatch Work Order will be denied.

Auto-Consume
Batch Materials

This configuration key defines the behavior of the Auto-Consume Batch Materials toggle in
the Operator Landing page. If this checkbox is selected, automatic generation and
consumption of batch materials is enabled by default for the whole production.
You can disable it centrally using the configuration key or you can use the toggle in the
Operator Landing page to disable it only for the Work Order Operation in execution.

Global
automatic NonConformance
close upon
Scrap

If the checkbox is selected, whenever a Non-Conformance is set to Scrap and is associated
to one of the following entities:
Work Orders
Tools
Material Tracking Units
Equipment
thereby causing the entity to be scrapped, the system automatically closes all the other
Non-Conformances associated to the entity.

Default Barcode
Rule

The default Barcode rule to be used to validate the Material if there are no rules set.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Import Work
Order Default
Status

This key is meaningful solely in contexts of integration with Opcenter Connect MOM in
which no value has been set for attribute Status in the XML file.
Used to import Work Order scenarios either from Master Plan or As Planned.
The value of this configuration key is used to set the imported Work Order's status.
Possible options:
NEW (default)
EDIT
READY FOR SCHEDULING

Runtime Failure
Association
Management

Failures can be associated with Materials and Equipment, and this is used to give a specific
context to those failures, i.e. to restrict their usage only to specific Materials and/or parts of
the plant.
This configuration key is used to specify what is the behavior in case Failures are
contextualized to specific Materials or Equipment. So, while creating a Non-Conformance
you can browse only those Failures that are applicable.
Possible values:
AND: If the key is AND, then during Non-Conformance creation, only those Failures will
be displayed for which both Material and Equipment of the Non-Conformance context
match with the ones associated with the Failure, where applicable.
OR: If the key is OR, then during Non-Conformance creation, only those Failures will be
displayed for which either Material or the Equipment of the Non-Conformance context
match with the ones associated with the Failure, where applicable.
For example, if the context is Material Tracking Unit only the Material would be considered
as a filter for the Failures, while if the context is Work Order Operation both produced
Material and Work Order Operation equipment are considered as filters according to the
possible value of the configuration key.

Complete Serial
Number(s)
without pausing
others

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
(Only for Serialized and FlexibleSerialized production types) If this checkbox is selected,
only the selected Serial Numbers will be completed for a specific Work Order Operation,
while the remaining Serial Numbers remain active along with the Work Order Operation.
Otherwise, the remaining Serial Numbers will be paused.

Auto-Select
First Document

If the checkbox is selected, when Documents are previewed from the Operator Landing
page, the focus is set on the first document in the list and its content is also loaded.

Automatic
Redirect to
details

If the checkbox is selected, the system automatically displays the Work Order Operation
details, after it has been started.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Check Work
Instruction Data

If the checkbox is selected, before completing Work Order Operations or Work Order Steps,
the system checks whether all the linked Work Instructions have been acknowledged and
mandatory Data Collections have been acquired.
If the mandatory Data Collections have not been acquired, Work Order Operations or Work
Order Steps cannot be completed.

Check Material
Consumption

If the checkbox is selected, Work Order Operations can be completed only when all required
Materials have been assembled. For Materials to be produced (where the Material
Specification is of type CoProduct or ByProduct), the Work Order Operation can be
completed only when all the Materials have been produced.

Enable Creation
New MTU From
Disassembled
Part

If the checkbox is selected, during a Disassemble, a new MTU will always be created from
the portion that has been disassembled from the Parent MTU.

Pause/Complete
Work Order
Operation by
Different User

Used for configuring Workcenters and/or Machines so that the Work Order Operations
pending on those pieces of Equipment can be paused or completed also by Users who did
not start them.
Possible values:
• YES: If this option is selected, the Work Order Operation can be paused or completed by
any User, even if the User did not start the Work Order Operation.
• YES with standard behavior: If this option is selected, the Work Order Operation can be
paused or completed by any User who is not active on the Work Order Operation. The User
who is active on the Work Order Operation can pause or complete only for him/her, but not
for the other users active on the Operation.
• NO: If this option is selected, the Work Order Operation must be paused or completed by
the User who started it.

Create Mat. on
Custom Mat.
Spec. Type
Consumption

Filter by
Preferred
Machine

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
If the checkbox is selected, in those cases in which the Material Tracking Units to be
consumed are Custom Material Specification Types and no such Serial Numbers or Batch
IDs are available for material consumption, the required Material Tracking Units of
the Custom Material Specification Types can be created on-the fly.
If this checkbox is selected, the Operator Landing page applies a filter on the Work Order
Operations according to the preferred machine set on Work Order Operations or Execution
Groups.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Show Execution
Groups in
Operator
Landing page

If the checkbox is selected, Execution Groups, in addition to the Work Order Operations, will
be displayed in the Operator Landing page.

Use Web
Workers for
data retrieval

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
If this checkbox is selected, data in the Operator Landing page is retrieved in an
asynchronous manner (by using Web Workers). In this modality, the Busy indicator is not
displayed while data is being loaded.

Filter by Users
assigned to
Work Order
Operations

If this checkbox is selected, the Operator Landing page applies a filter on the Work Order
Operations according to the logged User.

Enable Future
Hold on
dependent
Work Orders

If this checkbox is selected, whenever a Non-Conformance is declared on a Work Order
Operation that is dependent on another Work Order, the status of the last Work Order
Operation will be automatically set to Future Hold.

Filter Work
Order
Operations by
logged User and
show free Work
Order
Operations

If this checkbox is selected, and you are searching for a specific Work Order using the Quick
Search, the Operator Landing page displays by default the Work Order Operations
assigned to the logged User, as well as any Work Order Operations, related to that Work
Order, that are not assigned to any user. If no Work Order Operations have been assigned to
the logged User, then only those Work Order Operations, that are not assigned (that is, the
ones that are free) will be shown.
If you are not using the Quick Search to find the Work Order Operations related to a specific
Work Order, then all the Work Order Operations assigned to the logged user are displayed,
independently from any Work Order. As such, the behavior determined by the Filter by
Users assigned to Work Order Operations configuration key prevails.
For performance reasons, if both the Filter by Users assigned to Work Order
Operations and Filter WOOps by logged User and show free WOOps
configuration keys are selected, the behavior of the first configuration key always
prevails.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Threshold for
Inspection
indicator (value
in percentage)

Allows the system to show an indicator on the Work Order Operations tiles in the Operator
Landing page, to notify the Operator that a quality inspection is approaching.
The indicator is shown:
for Part Based inspections, when the percentage of items to be produced that is
missing to reach the configured inspection frequency.
for Unit Based inspection, when the percentage of unit of pieces to be produced that is
missing to reach the configured inspection frequency.
For example, in case of Part Based and Unit Based inspection, if this value is set to 10 and
the frequency of the inspection has been set to:
10, the alert is displayed when 9 items are produced,
100, the alert is displayed when 90 items are produced.

PCD Refresh
Interval

Refresh time (in seconds) of the Production Coordinator Dashboard and the Work Order
Network.
This key is also used to refresh the Work Order Routing.

Refresh Time
WIP
Show Users
having
appropriate
skills

Refresh time (in seconds) of the Work Order Work in Progress page.

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI
Application.
If this checkbox is selected, the Production Coordinator Dashboard displays only the Work
Order Operations associated to users with the appropriate skills.

Show only Work
Order Operation
ready to be
started

If the checkbox is selected, when the Operator selects a Work Order in the Operator
Landing page, the system displays only those Work Order Operations that can be executed.

NonConformance
Context on
Quality
Inspection

Used to define the context for the Quality Inspection Non-Conformance. During the
execution of a Quality Inspection, in case a Failure is detected, then depending on how this
configuration key has been set, the Non-Conformance will be created either on the Material
Tracking Unit or the Work Order Operation.
Possible values:
Material Tracking Unit
Work Order Operation

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuration
Key

Description

Prefix for
Rework Work
Orders

By setting this configuration key, whenever a Quality Non-Conformance is opened on a
Work Order Operation or Material Tracking Unit as requiring reworking, the Work Order that
is subsequently created will bear a specific prefix (default: RWK-) so that it can be
distinguished from the other Work Orders.

Pause Work
Order on scrap
NonConformance

By enabling this configuration key, when a Non-Conformance is sentenced as scrap, the
related Work Order will be set in Pause status instead of New status (when all active serial
numbers have been scrapped) in order to highlight that it has already been partially
worked.

Skip Quality
Inspection
automatically

By enabling this configuration key, when Material Tracking Units are completed on a
Referenced Operation of a Quality-only Inspection Operation, if the threshold of pieces to
be inspected has not been reached, the Material Tracking Units will be automatically
skipped so that they are available on the next Work Order Operation in the routing.

Containment
Request System

Here it is possible to provide a specific Name to identify the current System involved in the
management of the Containment Requests, which can, possibly, be imported from an
External System.

#### 3.1.3 Result Configuration Settings

The following General Configuration Settings are related to Result functionalities.
In order to use Results properly, it is mandatory that you have configured at least two Result Types: that is,
one must be dedicated to NOK and set to True, whereas the other must be OK set to False. Both must
have the IsDefault flag enabled.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configura
tion Key
Result
Managem
ent Type

Description

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application and
it is not applicable in the Operator Terminal for Repetitive Manufacturing.
Selecting a value from this list determines whether Result management is enabled at Operation or
at Activity level.
Possible values:
OFF: Result management is disabled, both for Operations and for Activities. No other
configuration keys are displayed.
Operation Result: Result management is enabled at Operation level. The following
configuration keys are displayed:
Result Management
Result Strategy Management
Activity Result: Result management is enabled at Activity level. The following configuration
keys are displayed:
Activity Strategy for Material Spec. Type
Activity Strategy for Quality Inspection Spec. Type
Activity Strategy for Tool Spec. Type
Activity Strategy for Work Instruction Spec. Type.

Result
Managem
ent

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application.
Selecting a value from this list determines:
Whether Result management at Operation level is enabled or not.
The default value that is set for what is displayed in the Complete Work Order Operation
panel, which displays the result at Operation level.
Possible values:
OFF: Result management is disabled.
OK: Result management is enabled: in this case, OK is selected as the default value for the
Result column displayed in the Complete Work Order Operation panel.
NOK: Result management is enabled: in this case, NOK is selected as the default value for the
Result column displayed in the Complete Work Order Operation panel.
N/A: Result management is enabled: in this case, no default value is selected for the Result
column displayed in the Complete Work Order Operation panel.
Once Result management has been enabled, if the Result Types provided by default
in Opcenter Execution Discrete do not satisfy your needs, you can configure custom
Result Types.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configura
tion Key
Result
Strategy
Managem
ent

Description

If Result Management is set to OFF, this configuration key is not available. It is not
available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application as well.
The Result Strategy Management key works in combination with the Result Management key.
Selecting a value from this list makes it possible to determine the corrective actions to be adopted
by default when result management is enabled.
Possible values:
Repeat: The failed Work Order Operation will be re-executed for the Batches or Serial Numbers
(MTUs) involved and then re-evaluated by the Shop-Floor Operator to determine whether this
second attempt was completed successfully or not. Should the Result still be deemed NOK,
the process of re-execution and re-evaluation will be re-iterated until the Result is deemed OK.
Repeat Existing Work Instruction: The failed Work Order Operation will be re-executed for
the Batches or Serial Numbers (MTUs) involved and then re-evaluated by the Shop-Floor
Operator to determine whether this second attempt was completed successfully or not.
Should the Result still be deemed NOK, the process of re-execution and re-evaluation will be
re-iterated until the Result is deemed OK. Already existing Work Instructions are repeated and
not created from scratch.
N/A: Although it is still possible to declare a Result, no consequent strategy (corrective action)
will be adopted.

Activity
Managem
ent

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application.
Selecting a value from this list determines whether Activity management is enabled or not.
Possible values:
OFF: Activity management is disabled. Activities are not created and the result of the Work
Order Operation is set at Operation level.
ON: Activity management is enabled. The result of the Work Order Operation is set at Activity
level. During the Work Order configuration, multiple Activities are created for each
Specification Type associated with the Work Order Operation, that is each Material to be
consumed, Tool to be used, Inspection Definition, Work Instruction Definition.

Activity
Strategy
for
Material
Spec.
Type

This configuration key is available only if the Activity Management key has been
enabled and it is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application.
Defines the strategy to be adopted for the Material specification type, in case the activity result is
NOK.
Default value: N/A. In this case, no automatic strategy will be adopted and the operator must
manually repeat the activity.

Applying General Configuration Settings

tion Key
Activity
Strategy
for

n Spec.

Defines the strategy to be adopted for the Quality Inspection specification type, in case the activity

Default value: N/A. In this case, no automatic strategy will be adopted and the operator must

#### 3.1.4 Additive Manufacturing Configuration Settings

The following General Configuration Settings are related to Additive Manufacturing functionalities.
Configuratio
n Key

Description

Check
Powder
Material
Recycle
Count

Select the checkbox if you want the system to validate the value of Current recycle count
against that of Maximum Recycle Count. Current recycle count represents the number of
times a recycle operation is performed on a Powder Material batch. With each recycle
operation, the system increments the value of Current recycle count. By selecting this
checkbox, the system is configured to validate that Current recycle count does not become
greater than Maximum Recycle Count.

Edit Print Job
File

Used for managing and editing values of a specific Print Job File.
Select the checkbox to enable the editing of the Print Job File details inherited from the CAM
system.

How to Configure the Production Environment
Applying General Configuration Settings

Configuratio
n Key

Description

Auto Print
Job File

Select the checkbox to manage Additive Manufacturing operations without importing Print
Job Files locally.
Clear the checkbox to manage Additive Manufacturing operations with Print Job Files
imported locally through a dedicated plugin. This is the default behavior.

Check
Substrate
Thickness

If you select this checkbox, the system validates the thickness of the Substrate against the
minimum thickness threshold. If the Substrate's thickness decreases below the threshold
value that has been set while configuring the Substrate, the Substrate's status is set to On
Hold and it is not available for production.

Check
Substrate
Treatment
Count

If you select this checkbox, the system will validate the treatment count value. If the treatment
count value rises above the maximum treatment count threshold that has been set while
configuring the Substrate, the Substrate's status is set to On Hold, and it is not available for
production.

PJF Import
Plugin

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application
and it has been deprecated in the Siemens_SIT_UADM UI Application.
The identifier of the plugin used to import Print Job Files, followed by the physical path of the
plugin file (Plugin Path text field).

PJF Transfer
Plugin

This configuration key is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application
and it has been deprecated in the Siemens_SIT_UADM UI Application.
The identifier of the plugin used to transfer Print Job Files, followed by the physical path of the
plugin file (Plugin Path text field).

The E-Mail tab is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application.
The following General Configuration Settings are related to the configuration of the server that must be used to
send e-mail notifications whenever a Transition of Quality Non-Conformances occurs. E-mails will be sent to any email address that has been properly configured within the corresponding Transition.

used.

Key

Description

Sender of NonConformance

Text for NonConformance
e-mail
message

The customized message contained in the e-mail notification that is sent by the SMTP server.

The SMTP server that the system uses for sending e-mail notifications for NonConformances. For example, smtp.acme.com.

SMTPS
(implicit SSL)

Selecting this check box indicates that the Simple Mail Transfer Protocol Secure (SMTPS)
protocol is used to secure e-mail communication at the transport layer. If you select this
checkbox, the default value of SMTP server port will be 587. If you do not select this
checkbox, the default value of SMTP server port will be 25.

SMTP server
port

The port that the SMTP server uses to send e-mails. If this key is left blank, the default value
will be 25, provided that the SMTPS (implicit SSL) checkbox is not selected. Instead, if the
SMTPS (implicit SSL) checkbox has been selected, the default value for SMTP server port
will be 587.

SMTP
authentication

The password for the SMTP account.

authentication

#### 3.1.9 Certification Configuration Settings

The Certification tab is not available in the Siemens_OPC_EXDS_AM UI Application.
The following General Configuration Settings are related to Certifications.
Configur
ation Key

Description

Certificat
ion
Manage
ment

Used for defining how the certifications must be verified at runtime. Possible values:

Validate
Certificat
ions
accordin
g to
Skills

Used to configure how the system must evaluate the Skills associated to a Certification.

Enable
Certificat
ion Skill
Level
Filter

Used to configure how the system validates the level of the Skills associated with the Certification.
This key is meaningful only if the value of the Validate Certifications according to Skills key is set
to either OR or AND.

AND: The system checks for valid certification for both Materials and Equipment Type. When
this value is selected, The User who starts, pauses or completes a Work Order Operation must
possess a valid certification for the Final Material produced by the Work Order and a valid
certification for the Machine used by the Work Order Operation. Furthermore, the Skills owned
by the User, if required, are checked according to the value of the Validate Certifications
according to Skills key.
OR: The system checks for valid certification for Materials or Equipment Type. When this value
is selected, the User who starts, pauses or completes a Work Order Operation must possess a
valid certification for the Final Material produced by the Work Order or a valid certification for
the Machine used by the Work Order Operation. Furthermore, the Skills owned by the User, if
required, are checked according to the value of the Validate Certifications according to Skills
key.
NO: The system does not check for any certification. Consequently, Skills are not checked.

NO: The system validates the Certification regardless of the associated Skills.
OR: The system validates the Certification if at least one of the associated Skills is verified.
AND: The system validates the Certification only if all associated Skills are verified.

NO: The system does not check the skill level.
EQUAL: The Certification is valid when it includes a Skill with level equal to the Skill level
required by a Work Order Operation.
EQUAL OR GREATER THAN: The Certification is valid when it includes a Skill with level greater
than or equal to the Skill level required by a Work Order Operation.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Define the Plant Structure

### 3.2 How to Define the Plant Structure

The first step in the configuration of the production environment is the definition of the plant structure, in which
you create a virtual representation of the plant by creating a set of Equipment and organizing them within a
hierarchical structure.
The procedure below indicates the steps required for the creation of a Plant structure. Complex configurations are
possible: for more information, see Equipment App in the Opcenter Execution Foundation User Manual.
If you decide to arrange Equipment Configurations in a Hierarchy, you should follow the recommended
configuration as described in section Business Plant Model: Equipment Levels and Machines of the Opcenter
Execution Discrete Product Overview. This configuration guides you in the association of the Machine to be used at
runtime during Work Order Configuration, by filtering out those that are not related to the linked Site.
The following situations are possible:
A single Hierarchy designed according to the recommended configuration: a filter is applied and only those
Machines related to the linked Enterprise/Site will be listed.
No Hierarchy, but flat Equipment Configurations are available: no filter is applied and you can associate any
Machine of your choice
More than one Hierarchy having the same Equipment Configurations of type Enterprise/Site: no filter is applied
and you can associate any Machine of your choice.

Target User
Users with the Production Coordinator or Product Engineer role can perform this action.

Workflow
1. Create Equipment Configurations.
2. (Optional) Configure the Equipment Hierarchy.

#### 3.2.1 Creating Equipment Configurations

While defining the Plant structure, the first step is to create the entities that hierarchically represent the pieces of
physical Equipment. Each piece of Equipment is an Equipment Configuration. All Equipment Configurations may be
then organized in an Equipment Hierarchy to represent your plant.
If anomalies or defects are discovered on existing Machines, it is possible to declare Quality Non-Conformances on
them, either by an Operator at runtime or by a Production Coordinator.
Equipment Configurations can also be associated to Automation Node Instances, to benefit from integration with
the Shop-Floor: for example, for the semi-automatic acquisition of values or for driving the execution behavior for
Tools and Materials.
For more information on Automation Nodes, see How to Manage Automation Nodes of the Opcenter Execution
Foundation User Manual..
For more information on how to associate Equipment Configurations to Automation Node Instances, see section
How to Configure the Association between Equipment and Automation Gateway of the Opcenter Execution Foundation
User Manual
The procedure below provides step-by-step instructions on how to create all Equipment making up the production
plant, including 3D Printers that, in addition, can also be created from the 3D Printers page.
In case of integration with the Shopfloor, you can see list of all Material Tracking Units that passed through
the Equipment on MTU Location History tab.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Define the Plant Structure

Procedure
1. Click

Product and Production Configuration > Equipment Configurations in the sidebar.

2. Click
Add Equipment Configuration.
3. Set the following parameters:
Parameter

Description

Id

The unique identifier assigned to the Equipment Configuration. Once
the Equipment Configuration is created, the Id value cannot be modified.

Name

(Optional) The name assigned to the Equipment Configuration.

Description

(Optional) Additional information on the Equipment Configuration.

Equipment
Type

(Optional) The Equipment Type to be used to define the Equipment Configuration content.
Once the Equipment Configuration is created, the Equipment Type value cannot be
modified. If the parameter is not selected, the system automatically uses the Equipment
Type that is currently set as default, and if the default Equipment Type does not exist, the
system creates an empty Equipment Configuration.
If you are configuring a Machine, select the UADM Equipment Type, to
automatically assign the default properties to the Equipment Configuration. The
UADM Equipment Type also provides the UADMEquipmentLifecycle state
machine.

Level

The position of the Equipment Configuration in the hierarchy. According to the selected
parent Equipment, either all or a subset of the following options are displayed:
Enterprise: the upper level, it represents the entire company.
Site: may represent a physical plant or a factory owned by the company.
Plant: may represent a physical plant or a factory owned by the company.
Area: may represent a sector within a factory.
WorkCenter: may represent a Workcenter and is focused on a specific type of
production.
ProcessCell: may represent a Workcenter and is focused on a specific type of
production.
ProductionLine: may represent an assembly line.
Segment: may represent a part of an assembly line.
Workplace: may represent a workstation.
StorageZone
WorkUnit
Unit: may represent a workstation where a single operation is performed (for
example, door stamping in a car manufacturing plant).
WorkCell
StorageUnit
ProductionUnit.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Define the Plant Structure

4. Click Save.
5. Repeat steps 2-4 for each piece of Equipment to be added to your plant.
6. In case of Equipment Configurations mapped to the Unit level, if you have associated the UADM Equipment
Type, click the Properties tab, to configure the following default properties according to your needs:

Is3DPrinter

If the value is set to True, the piece of Equipment represents a 3D
Printer to be used in Additive Manufacturing.

the Print Job File.
Note This parameter can also be set while configuring the 3D
Printers.

(only if Is3DPrinter is True)

The name of the Plugin that manages the Print Job File transfer.

this piece of equipment can be completed by a user differing from

User configuration key.

If the value is set to True, the Equipment associated to a Work

of the Operation and will be unavailable for concurrent use.
Equipment that has been locked can be unlocked manually. From
the State Machine tab of the Equipment Management page,
change the Equipment status clicking the Set Equipment Status
button and setting the Status Transition Verb parameter
to from Busy to Ready.
Equipment of Unit level does not inherit the setting of
this parameter from its Workcenter parent Equipment.

IsActive

If the value is set to True, the Equipment is active.

#### 3.2.2 How to Design the Equipment Hierarchy

Once created, the Equipment Configurations may be arranged in a Hierarchy in order to be used in production.

Workflow

2. Configure the Equipment Hierarchy.

How to Configure Numbering Patterns

Creating the Equipment Hierarchy
1. Click

Product and Production Configuration > Equipment Hierarchy Configurations in the sidebar.

2. Click

Add Equipment Hierarchy Configuration and set the following parameters:

r

Description

on

Configuring the Equipment Hierarchy
1. In the Equipment Hierarchy Configurations page, select the previously-created Equipment Hierarchy from the
list on the left.
2. Do either of the following:
If the Equipment Hierarchy is empty, click
Add Node in the toolbar.
If the Equipment Hierarchy already contains Equipment, select the node below which you want to add
other Equipment.
3. Select the Equipment of interest.
4. Click Save.
5. The selected Equipment are displayed as a flat list below the previously-selected node (or at the root level, if no
node had been selected): organize the Equipment hierarchically dragging and dropping them to the proper
position. When you move a node, also the underlying nodes are automatically moved.

### 3.3 How to Configure Numbering Patterns

Opcenter Execution Foundation provides the possibility to configure Numbering Patterns, and Opcenter
Execution Discrete extends this functionality, allowing you to define them for specific types of entities. As
some of the provided options are not relevant or applicable in the Discrete context, the current
documentation only provides information on steps necessary for configuring Discrete Numbering
Patterns. Only involved parameters, buttons and tabs are mentioned.
Each entity within the system is characterized by a unique identifier: you are prompted to define it while configuring
the items involved in your production processes.
In addition, while configuring specific entities (for example, Serial Numbers, Material Batches and Work Orders), you
can choose to either insert the identifier manually or generate it automatically according to a predefined pattern.
To define unique Numbering Patterns, it is necessary to create a Counter, which is one of the parts making up the
Numbering Pattern. It allows you to generate an increasing sequence of positive integers that can be incremented
or reset according to predefined configuration values. The same Counter can be used in more than one Numbering
Pattern.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Numbering Patterns

The definition of a Numbering Pattern consists of two phases, which can be performed at different times. In the first
phase, you create the pattern, but, at this point, it is simply a container that cannot be used to generate unique
identifiers. The final structure of the identifiers is defined during the second phase, in which you specify the
sequence of text chunks (called Numbering Pattern Parts) to be used to form the identifier.
For more information on Numbering Patterns, see How to Manage Autonumbering in the Opcenter
Execution Foundation User Manual.
Numbering Patterns can be deleted (
) even if they have already been associated to a Material, but the
association is not automatically deleted and it must be removed from the Numbering Pattern tab of the related
Material.

Default Numbering Patterns
The following Numbering Patterns are provided by default and they are used to generate the identifiers of the
messages exchanged by the system:
ImportDataFlowStatus, used for Import Data Flows.
InputMessageStatus, used for Input Messages.
KanbanCall, used for Kanban Calls.
JIT-JISMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type JIT/JIS Message.
KanbanMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type Kanban Message.
OperationOutputMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type WO Operation
Output Message.
StandardOutputMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type Standard Output
Message.
WorkOrderOutputMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type Work Order
Output Message.
AsBuiltMessage, used as default Numbering Pattern for Output Messages of type As Built Output Message.
In addition, the ChangePackage default Numbering Pattern is used to generate the identifiers for Change
Packages.
Do not remove these Numbering Patterns. If necessary, you can modify the structure of the Numbering Patterns
dedicated to Output Messages or create new ones (and then associate them with the proper Output Message
Definition).

Target User
Users with the Product Engineer role can perform this action.

Workflow
1. Create the Counter.
2. Create the Numbering Pattern.
3. Add Numbering Pattern Parts.

Creating the Counter
1. Click the
Counters home tile.
2. Click
Add Counter.
3. Set the following attributes:

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Numbering Patterns

Attribute
s

Description

Id

The unique identifier of the Counter.

Name

The name assigned to the Counter.

Descripti
on

Additional information on the Counter.

Enforced
Sequenci
ng

If selected, enables the transactional behavior for counters. If the creation command fails, the
system rolls back the progressive value incremented by the Counter, thus reusing the number
after another attempt at executing the command has been made.
If not selected, the system does not roll back the progressive value incremented by the
Counter. thus further increasing the progressive number after another attempt at executing the
command has been made.

Seed

The starting value of the Counter. The value must be positive or equal to zero. The default
value is 0.

Incremen
t

The incremental value added to the previous value. Only positive values are supported. The
default value is +1.

Max
Value

The highest value to be reached before the Counter is reset to its seed. If not specified, it is
never reset and a possible overflow error is raised.

Leading
Zeros

If selected, enables zeros to be added before the first significant digit in a numbering string.
The default value is false.

Time
Based
Reset

Defines when the reset takes place. The possible date formats are:
yyyy, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar year.
MM, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar month.
ww, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar week.
dd, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar day.
yyyy-MM, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar yearmonth. (Deprecated)
yyyy-ww, the Counter is automatically reset on the start of a new calendar yearweek. (Deprecated)
The default value is an empty string.

4. Click Save.

Numbering Patterns home tile.

Name

Descripti
on

Adding Numbering Pattern Parts
1. In the Numbering Patterns page, select a Numbering Pattern and click
Open Numbering Pattern.
2. Click the Numbering Pattern Part tab.
3. Click
Add Numbering Pattern Part.
4. Set one of the allowed Numbering Pattern Parts (the others will be automatically disabled as they must be
configured one at a time):
Constant: the constant value string of your choice.
Counter: a progressive numeric value previously configured.
Parameter: the property of the entity whose value will be used in the Numbering Pattern. If the property
does not have a value then the Numbering Pattern will be created without property value. The available
options to choose a parameter vary according to the Entity Type to which the Numbering Pattern refers.
Custom: a custom Numbering Pattern Part which has been created inserting data in Dbinit. For more
information, refer to the Opcenter Execution Foundation documentation.
Timestamp: the string representing the point in time when this numbering pattern part is generated.
Choose one of the following options: yyyy (year), MM (month), ww (week), dd (days), hh (hours), mm
(minutes), ss (seconds), msec (3 digits milliseconds). The remaining options have been deprecated.
Common Functions: the common predefined regular expression. For more information, refer to the
Opcenter Execution Foundation documentation.
Regular Expression: the property of the entity whose value will be a regular expression and the value
will be applied to the selected Parameter and Custom Part. For more information, refer to the Opcenter
Execution Foundation documentation.
5. Click Save.
6. Repeat steps 3 to 5 to add all the required Numbering Pattern Parts to your Numbering Pattern.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Line Sequences

### 3.4 How to Configure Line Sequences

Line Sequences are specific types of Numbering Patterns, used to identify a Material Tracking Unit in the production
line, since it could be associated with different ERP Orders at the same time. For this purpose, once defined, Line
Sequences are used by the Milestone Attribute CALCULATE_LS of the Production Flow Control Diagram, to
automatically generate an identifier code for the MTU.
Line Sequences are configured from the Line Sequence Management page and, once created, are merely empty
containers for which you have to define the different parts described below, in any combination and in the
sequence you require:
Counter: a progressive numeric value,
Constant: the string representing the constant value of your choice,
Timestamp: the string representing the point in time when the pattern for the Line Sequence has been
generated,
Parameter: the properties of the MTU, whose values will be used within the Line Sequence.

Target User
Users with the Product Engineer role can perform this procedure.

Accessing the Working Environment
To access the Line Sequence Management page, click
Management in the sidebar.

Production Coordination > Line Sequence

Workflow
1. Create a Line Sequence.
2. Add Line Sequence Parts.

1. In the Line Sequence Management page, click the

button and then set the following parameters:

Parameter

Description

Name

The name to be assigned to the Line Sequence.

Description

(Optional) Useful additional information on the Line Sequence.

Production Line

Equipment of type Production Line for which you want to configure the Line Sequence.

2. Click Save. The Line Sequence is created as an empty container and you must now add its Parts.

Adding Line Sequence Parts
1. Select a Line Sequence and click the Numbering Pattern Part tab.
2. Click the
button and set one of the allowed Parts (the other three will be automatically disabled as they
must be configured one at a time):

How to Configure Barcode Rules

Counter

A progressive numeric value previously configured. In this case, the Counter will be used for
the Line Sequence, but more complex configurations are possible. Only counters for which a
transactional behavior has been configured, are displayed as drop down options.
For information on how to configure Counters, see the Reference App documentation in
the Opcenter Execution Foundation User Manual.
The drop-down menu displays a list of default counters, specific to Input/Output
messages and Import Data Flows, which must not be selected, since they are
automatically used by the system for functionalities not related to Line Sequences.

MaterialNId, the Identifier of the Material associated with the Material Tracking
Unit.
MaterialRevision, the Revision (version) of the Material associated with the
Material Tracking Unit.
The string representing the point in time when the pattern has been generated. The
available options are: yyyy (year), yyyy-MM (year-month) and yyyy-ww (year-week
number).

3. Click Save.

### 3.5 How to Configure Barcode Rules

Barcode Rules make it possible to validate a Barcode string against a specific, configurable rule.
Barcode Rules can be configured using Rule Parts in the SerialNumber/BatchId and Material combination, and a
regular expression which is used to validate the Barcode string.
To validate Barcodes at runtime, Barcode Rules can be associated to:
Material
Functional Code of the Material
There is also the possibility to define a default Barcode Rule:
associating it to the Final Product Type
setting a dedicated configuration key
At runtime, according to the specific Solution that has been configured, the Barcode validation behaves differently.
If no rules are associated to the Material or Functional Code, the default Barcode Rule is retrieved through two
mutually exclusive post-actions (that is, they must be alternatively enabled):

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Barcode Rules

GetDefaultConfigKey

bility

Available in Solution

U4DM

Retrieves the default Barcode
Rule saved in the Default
Barcode Rule configuration
key.

GetDefaultFptRule
OnePieceFlow
U4DM

OnePieceFlow
U4DM

Retrieves the default Barcode
Rule:
saved in the Default
Barcode Rule configuration
key.
associated to the Final
Product Type, only in case
of Traceability Operations
executed in the Operator
Terminal for One Piece Flow
Manufacturing.

In addition, it is possible to view the History of each Barcode reading executed at runtime to easily know which ones
are valid or not.
For information on further functionalities provided by Opcenter Execution Foundation, refer to the Barcode App
documentation in the Opcenter EX FN User Manual.

sidebar.

5. (Optional) Associate a Barcode Rule to a Final Product Type.
6. View Barcode History.

#### 3.5.2 Creating Rule Parts

After creating a Barcode Rule, you must define it by adding one or more Rule Parts. Each Rule Part is defined by a
regular expression that will be used to validate the received Barcode string at runtime.

Procedure
1. In the Barcode Rules page, select a Barcode Rule.
2. Click the Rule Parts tab.
3. Click

and set the required attributes:

Attribut
e

Description

Identifi
er

The identifier assigned to the Rule Part. Once the Rule Part is created, the Identifier value
cannot be modified.

Name

The name assigned to the Rule Part.

Descript
ion

(Optional) Additional information on the Rule Part.

Item
Type

(Optional) Represents the item which must be considered in the Barcode Rule.
The product offers two predefined item types which are used for traceability at runtime:
PartNumber - the identifier of the Material associated to the Barcode Rule.
SerialNumber - the code of the Material Tracking Unit associated to the Barcode
Rule.
It is not possible to set twice the same Item Type in the same Barcode rule.
If you do not select the item type, the Rule Part will contribute in validating
the rule but its content will not be returned in the generated string.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Barcode Rules

Attribut
e

Description

Prefix

(Optional) Represents the prefix to be included in the regular expression. For example, SN for
Serial Number.

Express
ion

Represents the regular expression that will be used to validate the received Barcode string.
For example:
\d{2} means that the matching Barcode must contain two numbers.
The following characters are currently not supported:
\r
\n
\t
The expression will be generated with boundaries (\b) at the beginning and at the end.

Suffix

(Optional) Represents the suffix to be included in the regular expression. For example: 00. The
Barcode provided must end with the numerals 00.

4. Click Save.

Example
Here is an example of the same Barcode matching or not matching the regular expression defined in the Rule:
Barcode

Regular Expression

Result

SN12NSPN12NP

\bSN\d{2}NSPN\d{2}NP\b

True

SN123NSPN1234NP

\bSN\d{2}NSPN\d{5}NP\b

False

Result
In the Overview tab of the Barcode Rules page, if you add more than one Rule Parts to your Barcode Rule, it is
possible to view the sum of all the regular expressions in the Expression field.

#### 3.5.3 Associating a Barcode Rule to a Material

Once Barcode Rules are created, they can be associated to Materials and automatically retrieved at runtime, when a
string is received from a Barcode reader.
If a Rule has not been set for the Material, then it is automatically retrieved from either the Functional Code or the
Final Product Type.
At runtime, the Barcode received for consumption is scanned and the MTU can be consumed once the Barcode is
successfully validated.

Barcode Rules can also be associated to Functional Codes. In this manner, whenever a Barcode string is received at
runtime, if a Barcode Rule has not been set for the Material, the system retrieves the rule to validate the Barcode
from the corresponding Functional Code.

#### 3.5.5 Associating Barcode Rules to Final Product Types

When a Barcode string is received at runtime, the system automatically retrieves the Rule to validate it from the
Material, or alternatively from the Functional Code (if a Rule has not been set for the Material). If neither of them are
found, the Rule is retrieved from the rule associated to the Final Product Type.

The Barcode History page tracks the outcome of each Barcode reading executed at runtime, indicating also if the
Barcode string is valid and matches the Rule.

Attribute

The identifier of the Barcode.

How to Configure the Production Environment
How to Configure Final Product Types

Attribute

Description

Expression

The regular expression used to validate the Barcode string.

User

The user who performs the operation.

Created On
