Operation.
Note This is the default behavior.

For more information on how to enable/disable prechecks in Solution Studio, see section Configuring Command
Settings of the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.

Optional Work Order Operations in the Routing
If the Work Order Operation containing the Quality Inspection in the routing is optional, then it will behave like a
standard optional Work Order Operation.

Work Order Operations containing only Quality Inspections
If, in a routing, the Work Order Operation dedicated to the Part Based and Unit Based Quality Inspection does not
contain other manufacturing activities to be performed, the pieces/Serial Numbers for which it is not required any
inspection are automatically skipped and become ready for the next Work Order Operation according to the
configured Frequency Value.
In case the Work Order Operation is also Out of Route, only pieces/Serial Numbers which require inspection
according to the configured Frequency Value becomes ready on it.
For Time Based, the Work Order Operation containing only the Quality Inspection well behave as an optional Work
Order Operation.

Work Order Operations containing various Manufacturing Activities
If the Work Order Operations contain various manufacturing activities in addition to the Quality Inspection, each
piece/Serial Number must be started and completed.

Work Orders modified after their generation from a Bill of Process
(For Part Based and Unit Based only) If several Work Orders are created from the same Bill of Process, they will
inherit the Part Based or Unit Based Quality Inspection configuration.
However, should the Referenced Operation be changed for one of the Quality Inspections pertaining to the "sibling"
Work Orders after being generated, the counter is no longer shared for that particular Quality Inspection and it is
triggered separately for each of the modified Work Order Operations.

Part Based or Unit Based Inspections with the same Main Manufacturing Operation
set as Referenced Operation
If a Part Based or Unit Based Inspection Definition is linked to an Operation and the same Operation is specified as
Referenced Operation, then the counter will count the pieces to be inspected as soon as they are started on the
corresponding Work Order Operation and no Material Tracking Unit is skipped.

10.3.2.5 Disassembling Material Tracking Units
It could be necessary to disassemble a fully-assembled Material Tracking Unit in the following cases:

A defective piece has been found on the fully-assembled MTU and the Production Coordinator decides to
disassemble (remove) this defective piece from the produced MTU.
The Production Coordinator decides that a partial Disassembly is necessary to remove a portion of the fullyassembled MTU and reserve it for future re-assembly.
It is also possible to disassemble a partially-assembled Material Tracking Unit before the corresponding Work Order
Operation for its assembly has been completed. For example, while assembling an MTU that must consist of three
colored pieces (red, white, green), the Operator discovers that he has erroneously assembled two red pieces and
one white one. To rectify this situation, one of the red pieces must be removed and replaced with a green piece:
therefore, the Operator must perform a partial Disassembly.
When performing a Disassembly that involves a Material Tracking Unit to which one or more Documents
have been associated and the quantity to be disassembled is less than the quantity of the assembled MTU,
the MTU will be split and the resulting MTU will be automatically associated to the same Documents that
were associated to the initial MTU.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is not in status Hold or Verified.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step of interest.
2. Click the Previous Data button: the list of MTUs that are currently assembled for the Work Order Operation or
Step in question is displayed.
3. Choose the consumed Material Tracking Unit of interest and click
Material will be automatically updated.

Disassemble. The quantity of the

The overall background color of the selected MTU, as well as the background color of its contained
elements, will change: for details, see page Consuming or Assembling Material Tracking Units.

10.3.2.6 Scrapping Consumed Material Tracking Units and Material Tracking
Units to be Consumed
During production, if a defective piece is detected on a previously assembled Material Tracking Unit or on Material
Tracking Unit that has not been assembled yet and the Operator decides to remove this defective piece from the
produced MTU, it is necessary to scrap the Material Tracking Unit.
In addition, in case of Batch MTUs, it is also possible to scrap their quantity partially.
The scrapped MTUs can be moved to a Container.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is not in status Hold, Verified or Completed.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step of interest.
2. Click the Previous Data button: the list of MTUs that are currently assembled to the selected Work Order
Operation or Step is displayed.
3. Choose the consumed Material Tracking Unit of interest and click
Scrap.
4. (Optional) Select one or more Failures to be associated to the Material Tracking Unit to be scrapped.

5. (Only for To Be Consumed Material Tracking Units) Browse for the Material Tracking Unit or insert the Material
Tracking Unit code.
6. (Only for Batches) Enter the quantity to be scrapped.
7. (Optional) Browse for the Container or insert the Container code.
8. (Optional) Add a Comment.
9. Click Save.

10.3.2.7 Transferring Part Program
This procedure allows you to perform the transfer of a Part Program to a CNC machine during the execution of the
Work Order Operation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, search for the Part Program of interest.
2. Click:
This button directly triggers the transfer command for the Preferred Part Program assigned to the
machine.It is visible if a Preferred Part Program is available.
Allows navigation to a details page showing a tree view of the entire package structure. From the
detail page, it is possible to transfer multiple Part Programs at the same time, if needed.
Displays the full history of transfer operations executed on the equipment.

Dnc Items Details
By navigating to the details page, the tree displaying all the DNC items starting from the related package is shown.
From this page, three operations can be performed:
Selecting an item with the CanPreview value set to true enables the Preview button.
If the Part Program associated is a Document linked to the DNC Item, it will be displayed in full-screen mode
using the document viewer; otherwise, the Part Program will be downloaded locally.
Selecting one or more items with the CanTransfer value set to true enables the Transfer button. It is possible to
transfer multiple Part Programs.
Selecting multiple items enables the Download and Manual Transfer button. It will be possible to download
multiple items locally and then transfer them manually.
This activity is tracked in the history.

#### 10.3.3 Adding Notes to Work Order Operations

Operators can write notes regarding the execution of a Work Order Operation: this could be useful to other
Operators. Also, Production Coordinators can add notes to provide extra information about a Work Order
Operation.
Notes can be added to a Work Order Operation, as well as to a Work Order. Notes that are added to a Work Order
Operation refer only to that specific Work Order Operation. Instead, when a Note is added to a Work Order, that
same Note will also be added to all the Work Order Operations making up that Work Order.
The notification must be properly configured, in order to be triggered after a Note has been added: this results in an
increment of the value displayed in the badge associated with the Notes. At this point, it is possible to handle the
note in question: for example, the person who receives the notification can then acknowledge the note.

Target User

Users with the Operator, as well as the Production Coordinator role, can perform this operation.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is released.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Notes button.
The Notes panel is displayed: any pre-existing notes that refer to the selected Work Order Operation will be
shown as tiles therein.
3. Depending on whether you want to add your note to an Operation or a Work Order, select either the Operation
or the Work Order tab.
4. In the New Note box, type the notes that you want to add.
5. Click

Save.

Results
The note that has just been added will be displayed as a tile on the panel itself corresponding to the selected
Operation or Work Order tab.
The value shown in the Notes badge will increase by 1.
Any notes that have been added will be visible inside the Notes tab of the As Built page corresponding
to the selected Work Order.
Added notes are displayed based on Sort By, Created On (latest date), or Status (acknowledged).

Acknowledging Notes
To acknowledge a note, click the
Acknowledge button. At this point, an
indicator will be displayed
inside the tile corresponding to the selected note and the badge value associated with the Notes will decrease by 1.

#### 10.3.4 Placing Work Orders on Hold

The status of the Work Order will be on hold. You need to close the Hold Reason in order to start or resume the Work
Order Operation.

Target User
Users with the Operator role can perform this operation.

Prerequisite
At least one Hold Reason has been configured.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select a Work Order Operation in the New, Hold, Pause or Queue status.
2. Click
Hold.
3. Select a Hold Reason for the Work Order.
4. Click OK.

#### 10.3.5 Pausing and Resuming Work Order Operations

If you are an Operator and issues in production arise, or changes need to be made to operations, you can pause a
Work Order that is currently in execution.
Pause By Different User
When the Pause/Complete Work Order Operation by Different User configuration key is enabled,
then the Work Order Operation can be paused by any User, even if the User did not start the Work
Order Operation.
When any Work Order Operation is Paused, then the machine involved in the execution of the Work
Order is automatically set from Busy to Ready for all Production types. In this case, as a prerequisite,
parameter Lockable parameter of the Equipment must be set to True.

Target User
Users with the Operator role can pause a Work Order Operation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select a Work Order Operation in the Active status.
2. Click
Pause.
3. Select a Reason for pausing the Work Order.
4. Click OK: if Electronic Signature is not enabled, this is sufficient to pause the Work Order. Otherwise, proceed to
the next step.
5. (Only if Electronic Signature has been enabled) In the prompt that appears on screen, type your password, some
notes and then click Validate User. The system checks your credentials against those of the list of authorized
Operators:
If you pass validation, you are authorized to complete pausing the Work Order.
If you fail validation, you are not authorized to complete pausing the Work Order, but you may continue
to handle Work Orders or Work Order Operations that do not have dependencies on the Work Order that
you are not authorized to pause.

Result
The system sets the Work Order status to Pause: if you want to resume the Work Order Operation, click

Start.

Any Equipment initially locked by the Work Order Operation will be released after the Operation has been
paused.

#### 10.3.6 Adding Documents at Runtime

In addition to those added during the creation of a Work Order, Documents can be added to Work Order Operations
at runtime:
by users with the Operator role, during the execution of a Work Order.
by users with the Production Coordinator role, for present or past Work Orders.

Prerequisite
The Collect Document parameter has been set while creating the Work Order Operation.
The status of the Work Order Operations is Active.

Procedure for users with Operator role
1. In the Operator Terminal page , select a Work Order Operation.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Add Documents button: this opens the Add Documents page,
which displays a navigation wizard on the far left.
3. Click the Add Documents step of the wizard.
4. Do either of the following:
Search for documents already existing in the system and select one or more documents: a badge for
each Document selected is displayed next to the navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected
Documents area.
Click
, to browse the document not yet available in the system and then import it. For tablets and
mobile devices, using this button, it is also possible to take a picture of the Failure involved and then
import it.
5. (Optional and only if the Work Order is of type Serialized) Click the Select Material Tracking Units step of the
wizard and select one or more tiles representing the Material Tracking Units to be associated with the
document: these tiles are displayed directly under the search filter provided.
6. In the Summary section, review the information and click Submit.

Procedure for users with Production Coordinator role
1. Click the

Work Orders tile in the home page.

2. Select the Work Order of interest and click
As Built Order.
3. Click the Operations tab, and select the Work Order Operation to which you want to add documents.
4. (Optional, only for Serialized Work Orders) If you want to add documents to a specific Serial Number, select it in
the Serial Numbers tab.
5. Click the Collected Documents tab.
6. Click
Add.
7. On the Add Documents page, do either:
If a document you require is available in the list, select it, and click Add.
If a document is not available, click Import, and using the file explorer, select the document. On the Add
Documents page, update the Identifier if required, and click Save.

#### 10.3.7 How to Manage Non-Productive Activities

Non-Productive Activities include for example workspace cleaning, tool maintenance and training, and can be
managed directly from the Operator Terminal while managing Work Order Operations. In details:
Multiple Activities can be started or completed at the same time.
It is possible to start and/or complete Activities belonging only to the currently-logged User.
Start and/or end dates of the Activities are automatically set to the current time.
Activities cannot be deleted from the log.
In addition, Production Coordinators and Product Engineers can manage Non-Productive Activities of all Users from
the Users page.

Starting Non-Productive Activities
1. In the Operator Terminal page, click
N-P Activities on the vertical command bar. The Assigned NonProductive Activities panel displays the activities you have previously started.
2. Click Assign and Start.
3. In the Assign Non-Productive Activities panel, select one or more activities among those previously created.
4. (Optional) In the Context drop down list, select the context of the activity.

5. If you have selected a context, insert the context items according to option selected in the previous step.
6. Click Save.

Completing Non-Productive Activities
1. In the Operator Terminal page, click
N-P Activities on the vertical command bar. The Assigned NonProductive Activities page displays the activities you have previously started.
2. Select one or more activities and click Complete. The selected activities are logged as completed with the
current date and time.

#### 10.3.8 Closing Flexible Work Orders

During production execution, it may be necessary to close a Flexible Work Order before its full completion. This may
happen, for example if the Work Order had been created for a given work shift and consequently it is necessary to
close it until the end of that shift, even if some Work Order Operations have not yet been completed.
When a Flexible Work Order is closed, if there are Work Order Operations that need to be completed, they will be
closed together with the Work Order: in this case, a copy of the original Work Order is created to permit the
completion of those Work Order Operations that were still in progress, in order to produce the remainder of the
original Work Order.

Prerequisites
All the Work Order Operations of the Work Order need to be executed at least once.
The Work Order status is either Queue or Pause.
The Production Type of the Work Order is FlexibleBatch or FlexibleSerialized.

Target User
Users with the Production Coordinator role or the Operator role can perform this operation.

Procedure
1. Do either of the following:
In the Work Orders page, select the Work Order to be closed.
In the Operator Terminal or Operator Terminal details page, select one of the Work Order Operation
the Work Order to be closed consists of.
2. Click
Close Flexible Work Order.
3. Either insert the unique identifier of the Work Order in the edit box or click the Generate button to
automatically create it according to a predefined Numbering Pattern.
4. Click Save.

Result
The status of the Work Order changes to Complete.
Work Order Operations in status Open are set to NotExecuted, while Work Order Operations in status Partial or
Complete do not change.
The Work Order quantity is automatically adjusted by the system and you can no longer modify it.
A new Work Order related to the closed one is created as follows:
with Production Type TransferBatch, if the original Work Order was of type FlexibleBatch
with Production Type Serialized, if the original Work Order was of type FlexibleSerialized.
The new Work Order contains a copy of the Work Order Operations contained in the closed one, and their status
correspond to the status of the original Work Order Operations at the moment of the closure.
The not produced quantity is assigned to the new Work Order.

All completed and scrapped items are logged for the closed Work Order both in the As Built Report and in the
Genealogy page.
All in progress and not started items are logged for the closed Work Order both in the As Built Report and in the
Genealogy page.

#### 10.3.9 Scrapping Quantities from Work Order Operations and Work Order Steps

After releasing a Work Order, if an unexpected event occurs, it is possible to scrap some Quantities from any
Equipment on which the Work Order Operation or Work Order Steps have been started, thereby permitting its
completion, even though less quantity is produced than expected.

Target User
Users with the Production Coordinator role or the Operator role can perform this operation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal page, select a Work Order Operation or a Work Order Step and then click the Scrap
button.
2. (Optional) Select one or more Failures to be associated to the quantities to be scrapped.
3. Enter the quantity to be scrapped for at least one Equipment among those on which the Work Order Operation
or Work Order Operation Step have been started.
4. (Optional) Insert a comment.
5. Click Save.

### 10.4 Completing Work Order Operations

When executing Work Orders, you can complete Work Order Operations which have been previously started and
managed.
A Work Order is to be considered as completed when all its Work Order Operations are in a final status (either
the Complete or the Not Executed status).
During the creation of the Work Order to which the Work Order Operation to be completed belongs, it is possible to
define a set of Outbound Interlocking Checks to verify various conditions (for example, the assembly of all required
Materials, the completion of the related Steps). If at least one active Outbound Interlocking Check fails, the Work
Order Operation/Step will not be completed: an error message for each of the failed Outbound Interlocking Checks
is displayed to the Operator.

Constraints involving Work Order Steps
Completing Work Order Steps on Machines is not feasible if the Machines are in one of the following statuses:
OnHold
Repaired
Scrapped.
This behavior of the system is governed by the following pre-checks, which are enabled by default:
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnStartWOStepFullQty
CheckEquipmentHoldOnStartWOStepFullQty
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnStartWOStepSerialized
CheckEquipmentHoldOnStartWOStepSerialized
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnCompleteWOStepFullQty

CheckEquipmentHoldOnCompleteWOStepFullQty
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnCompleteWOStepSerialized
CheckEquipmentHoldOnCompleteWOStepSerialized
You can disable these pre-checks in Solution Studio as explained in section Configuring Command Settings of
the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation to be completed.
2. Click
Complete.
3. (Optional) If your Production Coordinator selected Electronic Signature Complete creating the Work Order
Operation, you are prompted to type your password, some notes and then to click Validate User.

### 10.5 Declaring Non-Conformances of Type Quality during Production

If a Failure was detected during production, you must open a Quality Non-Conformance.
Based on the settings of the Allow Opening a Non-Conformance on Parent Failure Configuration Key,
Non-Conformances can be created on Failures and their respective Sub-Failures in the hierarchy.
While creating Non-Conformances, Failures can be filtered with respect to Material or Work Order
Operation based on the Configuration Key Runtime Failure Association Management settings.

Prerequisite
The involved Work Order Operation is in status Open or Active.
The involved Failure is in status Current.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Declare NC button: this opens the Create Non-Conformance
page, which displays a navigation wizard on the left.
3. Select the tile(s) representing the Failure(s) involved in the Quality Non-Conformance that you want to open: the
Add Defect step of the wizard turns green and a badge for each Failure selected is displayed next to the
navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected Defects area.
4. Click the Add Details step of the wizard and then click one of the following tabs to select the context in which
the Failure has been encountered:
Work Order Operation
Material Tracking Unit
Equipment
Tool
5. Set the following parameters:
Parameter

Description

Identifier

Unique identifier to be assigned to the Non-Conformance. Once saved, this parameter can no
longer be modified.

Parameter

Description

Severity

The severity for the Failure. Possible values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

Comments

(Optional) Any additional information regarding the Failure observed.

6. Select one or more tiles representing the items involved in the Failure.
Only for Material Tracking Unit tab
This filter makes it possible to conduct searches on MTUs by Material Tracking Unit Code or Material
NId: by default, the Show only Material Tracking Units related to the selected Work Order
Operation toggle is set to true, thus allowing you to make your selections from among solely those
MTUs related to the selected Work Order Operation. If you want to make a selection from among all
MTUs (including those not related to the selected Work Order Operation), set this toggle to false so that
all MTUs are displayed for selection.
7. (Optional) Click the Add Documents step of the wizard. In the Documents tab, you can select the tile(s)
representing the Document(s) involved in the Quality Non-Conformance that you want to open: a badge for
each Document selected is displayed next to the navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected Documents area.
If the Document you want to add is not already in the list, do either of the following:
click

, to attach a document already present in the system.

click
, to browse the document of your choice and then import it. For tablets and mobile devices,
using this button, it is also possible to take a picture of the Failure involved and then import it.
8. In the Summary section, review the information and click Submit.

Results
A Non-Conformance is created in status Open.
The state of the last Work Order Operation in the routing changes from Open to Future Hold: the state indicator
changes to
. If the last Operation in the routing is in a state different from Open, its state changes directly
to Hold.
The nested card's background color used to represent the status of the activity changes to pale red.

### 10.6 Requesting Change Packages on a Work Order Operation or Work Order Step

At runtime, the Operator can notify the Production Coordinator that some changes must be made on-the-fly to the
Work Order Operation or Step (in particular, to the set of Specifications required to successfully execute that
specific Work Order Operation or Step). This is known as a Change Package request.

Prerequisites
Only one Work Order Operation has been selected and it belongs to the TransferBatch production type.
The involved Work Order Operation or Step is in status Open or Partial.
No other Change Packages have been requested for the involved Work Order Operation or Step.

Procedure

1. In the Operator Terminal, or Operator Terminal details select the Work Order Operation or Step (only if you
are operating inside the Operator Terminal details) of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Change Package button: a dedicated panel opens, where you can
insert information on the changes to be made to the Work Order Operation or Step.
3. Click Save. Once the Change Package has been saved, it can no longer be updated.

Results
A Change Package is created in status Editing.
The status of the involved Work Order Operation is changed to Hold: the status indicator changes to

.

Likewise, the
indicator is displayed only on the Work Order Operation (even if the Change Package was
created for the Work Order Step), indicating that a Change Package request has been opened either on the Work
Order Operation or one of its Steps.
On the Vertical Command Bar and in the Nested-Card Zone, the
Start,
Pause and
Complete
buttons are disabled.
On the involved Work Order Operation, no actions are allowed (for example, the adding of Materials, Documents
or Tools). The same behavior applies also to the related Steps.
It is not possible to open Non-Conformances of type Change on the involved Work Order Operation.

## 11 How to Execute Work Orders in the Operator Terminal for Manufacturing

The Operator Terminal for Manufacturing is recommended for Serialized, FlexibleSerialized, FullQuantity,
TransferBatch and FlexibleBatch production.

Main Workflow
Start Work Order Operations
Manage Work Order Operations
Display Document Files
Manage Activities for Work Order Operation/Step progression
Manage Work Instructions
Use Tools
Consume Material Tracking Units
Inspect Material Tracking Units
Disassemble Material Tracking Units
Add Notes to Work Order Operations
Place Work Orders on Hold
Pause and resume Work Order Operations
Add Documents at Runtime
Complete Work Order Operations

Additional Operations
In addition to the operations listed above, while executing a Work Order Operation, you may need to:
declare a Quality Non-Conformance during production
request a Change Package on the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step

### 11.1 Navigation of the Operator Terminal for Manufacturing

The Operator Terminal is the main environment in which you will move to carry out production execution. It is from
here that you will be able to start your Work Order Operations, as well as perform other activities.
Here is what you need to know to navigate this environment and interpret what is displayed on screen, as well as
adapt its contents according to your needs.

Indicators Visible on Tiles
The tiles presented in the Operator Terminal may contain dedicated indicators related to various aspects of
production execution.
Consult the legend below to comprehend their meaning.
The following indicators allow you to interpret the current status of a Work Order Operation displayed in tile format:
for a description of the corresponding states, see section Work Orders, Work Order Operations and Work Order
Steps in the Opcenter Execution Discrete Product Overview.
There are also some indicators that refer to other elements placed in relation to Work Order Operations: Work
Operations, Non-Conformances.
Indicator

Meaning
Open

Ready

Active

Partial

Hold

Future Hold
Complete
Not Executed
A Non-Conformance is present on the Work Order Operation.

A Change Package request has been opened on the Work Order Operation.

The Work Order to which the Work Order Operation belongs is on hold.

The Work Order Operation is of type Dynamic (that is, it belongs to a Work Order of FlexibleBatch
production type).

Indicator

Meaning
A Quality Inspection is approaching.

Material Tracking Unit (or Serial Number) Tiles
Indicator

Meaning
The MTU is ready to be started.

The MTU has been started.

The MTU has been paused.

The MTU has been completed.

Dependency Graph
The Dependency Graph is a graphical representation of the Dependencies that exist between the Work Order
Operations belonging to the selected Work Order (or Work Order Operations belonging to different Work Orders). Its
purpose is to guide users in managing runtime execution.
If desired, you can customize the Operation Dependency Graph to satisfy your needs:
To change the size of the graph, click to the left or right of the Zoom slide or use the mouse wheel.
To display the entire graph on a single page, click Auto Fit.
To change the position of the graph, select it and drag it.
If there are Operation Folders in the graph, double-click on them, to see their content.
To zoom into a node, just click it while holding the control (CTRL) key on the keyboard.
You can also click

, to view the Dependency Graph in fullscreen mode and click

, to exit.

Operations Available in the Search Bar
In the Operator Terminal page, do one of the following:
In the Work Order box on the left, type the Work Order ID (starting from the beginning of the string): the system
displays the Work Order Operations belonging to the Work Orders matching your search criteria.
In the Product box in the middle, type the full code of the MTU to be produced: the system displays all the Work
Order Operations that match your search criteria and can be started.
In the Machine box on the right, type the full name of the Machine: the system displays all the Work Order
Operations that match your search criteria and can be started.
To clear the search criteria applied for the filters, click the Clear All button.

Operations Available in the Vertical Command Bar

According to their prerequisites, some buttons might be disabled.
All the procedures listed below (except for the
Open operation) can be executed both from the
Operator Terminal page and from the Operator Terminal details page, by clicking the related buttons
in the Vertical Command Bar.
Click the following buttons:
Open to open the Work Order Operation.
Start to start the current Work Order Operation.
Pause to pause the current Work Order Operation.
Hold to hold the Work Order to which the current Work Order Operation belongs.
Complete to complete the selected Serial Numbers for the current Work Order Operation.
Declare NC to declare a Quality Non-Conformance.
Change Package to request a Change Package for the selected Work Order Operation (or Work Order Step
as well if you are operating inside the Operator Terminal details page).
Add Documents to associate documents, either new or already available in the system, to the Work Order
Operation (or Work Order Step as well if you are operating inside the Operator Terminal details page).
Notes to add and acknowledge notes related to the Operation or the Work Order.
Scrap to scrap Serial Numbers from Work Order Operations (or Work Order Steps as well if you are
operating inside the Operator Terminal details page).
Genealogy to display Historical Data about the Work Order in progress.
As Built to display Historical Data.
Freeze Target Quantity to set the desired Target Quantity in case of Work Operations of type Dynamic.
Close Flex WO to close a flexible Work Order.
N-P Activities to manage non-productive activities.
External Integration, to open an external system web UI with the Work Order Operation parameters.

### 11.2 Starting Work Order Operations

Executing a Work Order entails starting at least one Work Order Operation.
When starting a Work Order Operation, the main discriminant to keep in mind is the Production Type of the Work
Order to which it belongs. The Production Type determines the execution flow of the Work Order Operation.
Keep in mind that the Production Type of the Work Order, as well as other configurations, will affect what is
displayed on screen and the procedure that you must follow to start your Work Order Operation.
In addition, according to the configurations that have been performed during engineering, it is possible that you
may need to perform additional actions and take into account certain constraints to start the Work Order Operation
with success.
The automatic start will be successfully executed if no other Work Order Operations (of the same category)
are queued on the same Machine.

If the setting for the configuration key Certification Management is set to AND/OR, then in the Operator
Terminal, when an Operator selects a Work Order Operation, the system will restrict viewing of details of
the selected Work Order Operation if the Operator does not have a valid certificate.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation to start and then click
Start on the vertical
command bar.
2. Do either of the following:
(For TransferBatch and FlexibleBatch production types) In the Quantity field, enter the Material
quantity on which you want to start to work. For the FlexibleBatch production type and in case of Work
Order Operation of type Dynamic (
), you can define any quantity of interest, even greater that the
quantity required by the Work Order, until the quantity of the Work Order has not been frozen or you
have abruptly closed the Work Order.
(For Serialized production) If more than one Serial Number is present for the Work Order Operation, do
either of the following:
select the tiles representing the Serial Numbers (MTUs) that can be managed by the selected
Work Order Operation.
select the
Select / Deselect All check box, to include all the listed Serial Numbers (they are
displayed in groups of twenty).
(For FlexibleSerialized production) proceed in one of the following ways:
in the Associate or auto-generate Serial Number field, type the name of a new Serial Number
and click
to associate it.
in the Associate or auto-generate Serial Number field, type the name of an existing, not yet
associated and compatible Serial Number and click
to associate it.
type the number of Serial Numbers to be automatically generated and associated by the system
and click
.
select the Show available Serial Numbers toggle to be able to select a Serial Number already
available in the system and click

.

select the
Select / Deselect All check box, to include all the listed Serial Numbers (they are
displayed in groups of twenty).
3. If more than one Workcenters/Machines are present, the panel Select Equipment opens. Select the
Workcenter/Machine to be used to execute the Work Order Operation.
4. (Only if Electronic Signature validation has been enabled) Type your password, some notes and then click
Validate User.
5. Click Start.

What to do next
After starting a Work Order Operation, you can display its details and manage them. Later, you can complete its
execution.

### 11.3 Managing Work Order Operations

When executing Work Orders, you can perform various activities on a Work Order Operation previously started.
Runtime management of Work Order Operations is a complex procedure that depends on the various parameters
set during their creation. There is no set workflow that must be followed: it may consist of several independent
steps without a fixed order.

Accessing the Working Environment
1. In the Operator Terminal page, select one or more Work Order Operations of interest (belonging to the same
Work Order) and then click
Open on the vertical command bar.
2. (For Serialized and FlexibleSerialized production types) Select the tiles representing the Serial Numbers
(MTUs) that can be produced by the selected Work Order Operation.
3. Click Open. This opens the Operator Terminal details from which you can manage the selected Work Order
Operation. If the Work Order Operation is not active, you will see a grayed-out preview of the associated
specifications (for example, Materials, Tools, Work Instructions).

Environment Description

Header Bar - At the top of the screen. Displays the details of the current Work Order Operation.
Operation/Step List - On the left of the screen. Displays the list of the selected Work Order Operations with the
related Steps.
Nested-Card Zone - In the center of the screen, immediately after the Operation/Step List. Displays the
elements needed to perform the current activity.
Document Viewer - In the center of the screen, immediately after the nested-card zone. Upon clicking the
Documents button on the vertical command bar, the Document Viewer displays the Documents associated to
different entities involved in the current Work Order Operation or Step.
Vertical Command Bar - On the far right of the screen. Displays a set of buttons to perform various activities on
the current Work Order Operation or Step.

Operations Available in the Header Bar
The top Header Bar displays the details of the current Work Order Operation.
(For Serialized and FlexibleSerialized production types) If the selected Work Order Operation contains more than
one Serial Number, the Serial Number label is shown as a link, that, once clicked, allows you to switch to another
Serial Number from a dedicated pane. Once selected, the Operator Terminal will be refreshed accordingly,
allowing you to manage the production of the newly-selected Serial Number.

Operations Available in the Operation/Step List
If the selected Work Order Operation contains Steps, then the left zone will display the Work Order Operation as a
tile, with a stack of its Steps underneath it. By clicking the individual tiles, you can perform the following actions:
Start a Work Order Operation.

Pause a Work Order Operation.
Complete a Work Order Operation.
In addition, you can also:
click the
status.

Show All Operations icon (default) to display all Work Order Operations, regardless of their

click the
Hide Completed Operations icon to display only those Work Order Operations that have not yet
been completed (that is, their status differs from Completed).
click the
icon to open the Navigator panel. In this panel you can see see the Work Order Operations
Dependency Graph and interact with it. You can, for example, change its size or its position, select a Work Order
Operation to manage it in the center part. For more information on the Dependency Graph, see Navigation of
the Operator Terminal for Manufacturing.

Indicators Available in the Operation/Step List
The tiles presented in the Operation/Step List may contain one or more indicators that represent activities:
depending on the status of the element to which the tile refers (Operation or Step), the color of the individual
indicator changes.
The Quality Inspection indicator does not change color: therefore, it is not possible to interpret its status.
The table below illustrates the available indicators and how they are to be interpreted according to the color they
assume when their corresponding activity is performed.
Indicat
or

Corresponds to an activity
involving...
Work Instructions
Material Consumption

Color Code for Activity Status (valid for all activities except
Quality Inspections)
Colorless: Activity available.
Yellow (both background and border): Activity partially
executed.
Green (both background and border): Activity completed.

Tool Usage
Quality Inspections

Operations Available in the Nested-Card Zone
The content of the nested-card zone depends on the activity to be performed on the current Work Order Operation/
Step. According to the aspect of runtime behavior configured for the selected Work Order Operation (or Work Order
Step) that you want to manage, the content of the details area will vary. Refer to Managing Activities for Work Order
Operation/Step Progression for details.

Operations Available in the Document Viewer Zone
Upon clicking the
Documents button in the vertical command bar, the Document Viewer zone displays the
documents associated to the various entities involved in the execution of the current Work Order Operation/Step.

Operations Available in the Vertical Command Bar
According to their prerequisites, some buttons might be disabled.
Click the following buttons:
Start to start the current Work Order Operation.
Pause to pause the current Work Order Operation.
Hold to hold the Work Order to which the current Work Order Operation belongs.
Complete to mark the current Work Order Operation as completed.
Declare NC to declare a Quality Non-Conformance.
N-P Activities to manage non-productive activities.
Documents to view the Documents attached to the selected Work Order Operation.
Notes to add and acknowledge notes related to the Operation or the Work Order.
As Built to display Historical Data.
Genealogy to display Historical Data about the Work Order in progress.
Freeze Target Quantity to set the desired Target Quantity in case of Work Operations of type Dynamic
(i.e. no more quantity can be started).
Freeze Actual Quantity to set the desired Actual Quantity in case of Work Operations of type Dynamic
(i.e. actual quantity can be started, but no more quantity can be added).
Close Flex WO to close a flexible Work Order.
Change Package to request a Change Package for the selected Work Order Operation or Work Order Step.
Add Documents to associate documents, either new or already available in the system, to the Work Order
Operation or Work Order Step.
Scrap to scrap quantities from Work Order Operations.
External Integration, to open an external system web UI with the Work Order Operation parameters.

What to do next
After managing a Work Order Operation, you can complete it.

#### 11.3.1 Displaying Document Files

The Document Viewer makes it possible for users to view any Documents that have been attached to a Work Order
Operation/Step (including those Documents that belong to a Document Item, with the exception of the Repetitive
context) or to a related entity.
Document visualization depends on its file format: see Supported File Formats.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Step for which you want to view its attached
Document.
2. Operating on the Vertical Command Bar, click the
Viewer.

Documents button to hide or unhide the Document

3. The lower area displays the list of available documents (including those that belong to a Document Item),
grouped by the entities to which they are associated. Only those entity groups that contain documents are
displayed. Select the desired group according to the following list:
Operation for the documents associated to the current Work Order Operation.
Step for the documents associated to the current Work Order Step.
Runtime for the documents associated to the Serial Numbers started by the current Work Order
Operation.
Product for:
the documents associated to the Final Material produced by the current Work Order Operation.
the documents associated to the active Material Tracking Units produced by the current Work
Order Operation.
Parts for the documents associated to the Materials consumed by the current Work Order Operation.
Tools for the documents associated to the Tools used by the current Work Order Operation.
Machines for the documents associated to the Machines used by the current Work Order Operation.
Plant for the documents associated to the Plant of the current Work Order.
Inspection Parts for the documents associated to the Materials, which are used as Reference Material of
a Quality Inspection (relating only to Variable or Attributive Quality Characteristics).
4. Click the preview or the

icon to expand the document to full screen. Click

to switch to the list mode

(here you can find the size of the document and also download the document by clicking

).

Those Documents that belong to a Document Item will bear a chip with the name of all the Document
Items of its appurtenance. This chip is not available for the Repetitive context.

Supported File Formats
The Document Viewer supports the following file formats:
File Types

Supported File Formats

Non-video files with preview

.txt/.html
.svg
.jpg
.png
.pdf
.jt

Video files with preview

.mp4
.ogg
.webm

Other file formats, such as:
.doc
.csv
.xml
.zip
are not displayed by the Document Viewer, but can be downloaded.

#### 11.3.2 Managing Activities for Work Order Operation Step Progression

The value of the configuration key Result Management Type must be set to OFF.
In Opcenter Execution Discrete, dedicated activities make it possible for users to manage the principal elements
involved in Work Order Operation/Step progression with ease.
The available activities concern:
the Work Instructions (including precautions, warnings and special notes) related to the progression of the Work
Order Operations and Work Order Steps, with the possibility of runtime data collection.
the Tools to be used to execute a specific Serial Number during the progression of the Work Order Operation or
Work Order Step.
the Materials and the relative quantities that are to be consumed/assembled, disassembled, or scrapped for
producing a specific Serial Number.
the Quality Inspections to be performed following the completion of the Work Order Operation or Work Order
Step.
the Transfer of a Part Program to a CNC machine to enable execution of machining operations related to the
current Work Order Operation.
The aforementioned activities are carried out in parallel by default, but can be configured otherwise, if desired.
If the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step is not active, you will see a greyed-out preview of the available
activities.

Available Operations
Manage Work Instructions.
Use Tools.
Consume Material Tracking Units.
Manage Quality Inspections.
Disassemble Material Tracking Units.
Scrap consumed Material Tracking Units and Material Tracking Units to be consumed.

11.3.2.1 How to Manage Work Instructions
Work Instructions are specific instructions for the Operator that may require an acknowledgement or the insertion
of values.
Depending on the content of the Work Instruction (Acknowledge and/or DataCollection steps), the system
behaves differently.
Moreover, in the specific case of Work Instructions containing DataCollection steps, the following configurations
are possible:
Work Instructions associated to the entire Work Order Operation or Step;
Work Instructions associated to all the Serial Numbers produced by the Work Order Operation.
Data is collected automatically if you have configured the system in order to exchange XML messages
with Opcenter Connect MOM (Opcenter CN MOM). For more information, see the section How to
Integrate Opcenter Execution Discrete with Opcenter Connect MOM in the Opcenter Execution Discrete
Installation and Configuration Manual.
In case of integration with the Automation layer, you will be able to automatically acquire Data
Collection values. You can then decide whether to save these values or edit them manually.
In case of integration with Teamcenter, PDF files linked to Work Instructions can only be displayed, if
they are configured in Teamcenter with the following format: ...<object data=\"data:application/

pdf;base64..." (for example: "<div><object style=\"width: 98%; height: 60vh;\"
data=\"data:application/pdf;base64...).
Work Instructions can be managed at Work Order Operation or Step level.
If Electronic Signature is enabled on the Work Instruction then while completing Work Order Operation/
Step, Work Instructions should be signed with the required user's Electronic Signature, otherwise an error
message is displayed.
This behavior is independent of the Configuration Key - Check Work Instruction Data.
If Work Instruction data is not required to Complete Work Order Operation/Step, then Electronic Signature
is also not required before competing Work Order Operation/Step.
After acknowledging or confirming your Work Instruction, you can re-edit it (Re-Edit button) to edit the desired
values.

Prerequisite
Work Instructions are linked to Work Order Operations or Work Order Steps.

Available Operations
Acknowledging Work Instructions
Providing Values for Data Collections associated to an entire Work Order Operation/Step
Providing Values for Data Collections associated to all Serial Numbers of a Work Order Operation
Associating Work Instructions to specific Serial Numbers.

Acknowledging Work Instructions
Work Instruction Acknowledgement in CLM Integration
By default, Work Instructions without Data Collections that will be imported from Teamcenter
Manufacturing can be viewed by the user without the need to provide successive acknowledgement in
order to proceed with his/her tasks.
It is possible to render acknowledgement mandatory by unsetting the Work Instruction without
Acknowledge configuration key. For more information, see CLM Integration Configuration Settings.
1. In the Operator Terminal, while managing the current Work Order Operation, search for the Work Instruction of
interest.
2. Read the displayed Work Instructions.
3. Click Acknowledge.

Providing Values for Data Collections associated to an entire Work Order
Operation/Step
1. In the Operator Terminal, while managing the current Work Order Operation/Step, search for the Work
Instruction of interest.
2. Provide values as follows:
by typing them manually
by clicking the Acquire button (if Data Collection parameters are linked to the Automation Node
Instance parameters) for the Data Collections associated to the Work Order Operation/Step. Before being
confirmed, the values can be updated even if they have been acquired from the field

by clicking
Calculate or
Calculate All. These buttons will be only present if Formulas have
been configured to calculate their values.
3. Click Confirm to save the value.

Providing Values for Data Collections associated to all Serial Numbers of a Work
Order Operation/Step
1. In the Operator Terminal, while managing the current Work Order Operation/Step, search for the Work
Instruction of interest.
2. Provide values as follows:
by typing them manually
by clicking the Acquire button (if Data Collection parameters are linked to the Automation Node
Instance parameters) for the Data Collections associated to the Work Order Operation/Step. Before being
confirmed, the values can be updated even if they have been acquired from the field
by clicking
Calculate or
Calculate All. These buttons will be only present if Formulas have
been configured to calculate their values.
3. Click Confirm to save the value.

Associating Work Instructions to specific Serial Numbers
1. While managing the current Work Order Operation, if a Work Instruction of type On Demand has been
associated to the Work Order Operation/Step, the
Instructions to be associated on-the-fly.

button will allow you opening a panel to select Work

2. Click
Add Work Instruction.
3. Select the Work Instruction to be added.
4. Click Save.

11.3.2.2 Using Tools
This procedure allows you to use Tools during the execution of the Work Order Operation or Step.
In case of integration with the Automation Layer, you will be able to acquire values related to the Tool ID, the usage
time and the usage duration. You can then decide whether to save these values or edit them manually.
In case of integration with Opcenter Connect MOM, it is possible to automatically acquire values related to
the Tool ID, the usage time and the usage duration.
Tools in OnHold status or currently locked are not available for usage at runtime.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, search for the Tool of interest.
2. Click the Browse button to assign the Tool that must be used.
3. Set at least one between Usage and Duration parameters.
Usage is automatically pre-filled with Quantity 1 after the Tool has been selected if another quantity
has not been previously specified. If a Quantity has been previously specified, it must be greater than 0.
4. Click:
to use only one Tool.

to use multiple Tools at the same time.
(visible only if Tool Definition parameters are linked to Automation Node Instance parameters) to
automatically acquire the Tool, Usage, and Duration fields with respective values.
If the parameter Consumable had been set while creating the Tool Definition, the field Consumable is
set to Yes.
If the Tool has been defined as Lockable (either directly or through its Tool Definition), using it will set
its state as Locked.

Tracking Tool Usage
After the Tool has been used, you can see the list of all the Tools that have been used for the specific Tool Definition
(linked to the related Work Order Operation/Step) by clicking the Previous Data button. In addition, for each Tool,
the following information is displayed:
the number of times the Tool has been used for each transaction
the usage duration.

11.3.2.3 Consuming Material Tracking Units
This procedure allows you to consume Material Tracking Units during the execution of a Work Order Operation or
Work Order Step (for example, for assembly).
Assembly is feasible solely if the values of both the Serial Numbers (and/or Batch IDs) of the MTUs to be consumed
and their respective quantities are indicated.
A dedicated color code has been adopted to allow you to interpret the material consumption status at a glance.
For details on how to perform Disassembly, see Disassembling Material Tracking Units.
In case of integration with Opcenter Connect MOM or Automation Gateway (Shop Floor) it is possible to
automatically acquire values related to the Serial Number/Batch Id and Quantity.

Prerequisites
For semi-automatic acquisition of Material parameters, Serial Number/Batch Id and Quantity are mapped to
Automation Node Instance parameters.
For validation of scanned Serial Number Codes/Batch Ids against Barcode Rules, Barcode Rules have been
previously configured using the Barcode App provided by Opcenter Execution Foundation.
The Auto-Consume Batch Materials configuration key is disabled.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step of interest.
2. For each MTU to be consumed, specify the values for Serial Number/Batch Id and Quantity, consulting the
table below before proceeding:

Parameter

Description

Serial Number/Batch Id

For an existing Serial Number or Batch: enter the values
either manually or using a Barcode reader.
For a Serial Number or Batch that does not yet exist:
enter its value manually and the system will create it onthe-fly. If the Auto-Consume Batch Materials
configuration key is set to On, batches are automatically
consumed, be they already available in the system or
created on-the-fly.
If Material parameters are mapped to Automation Node
Instance parameters, click the
Acquire Material
button to automatically retrieve the Serial Number/

11.3.2.3.1 Assembling Pre-Kitted Material Tracking Units
When you consume or assemble pre-kitted Material Tracking Units, the system indicates what exactly is to be
consumed for a specific Work Order in terms of reserved Serial Number codes or Batch Ids.
If the pre-kitted Material Tracking Units to be consumed are Batches, the quantities will be displayed as they have
been pre-kitted over multiple Batches. The Material Tracking Units to be consumed may thus originate from
different Batches.
If you want to consume pre-kitted MTUs together with MTUs that have not been reserved, both types of MTUs will
be assembled when the Assemble button is clicked.
If the pre-kitted MTU to be consumed is not available, you can specify another MTU (be it a Serial Number Code or
Batch ID), thus changing what has been previously reserved. In addition, for Batches, it is possible to specify a
different quantity, provided that the new value is less than the original one.
If the quantity of the pre-kitted MTU is less than the quantity of the Material to be consumed (for the
specified target MTU), once the pre-kitted MTU has been consumed, you can then continue consumption
as usual until the total quantity of the Material to be consumed has been depleted.

Prerequisites
Material Tracking Units have been reserved (pre-kitted) for the Work Order in execution.

Available Operations
Changing Pre-Kitted MTUs
Validating and Assembling Pre-kitted MTUs
Validating and Assembling Pre-kitted MTUs using Barcode Rules

Changing Pre-Kitted MTUs
1. Click
Edit next to the Pre-kitted MTU you want to change.
2. Type the new Serial Number Code or Batch ID.
3. (For Batch ID only) Type a new value for the quantity, if necessary.
4. Click

Save to update the Pre-kitted MTU or click

Close editing mode to cancel the update action.

Validating and Assembling Pre-kitted MTUs
1. If the Material Tracking Unit to be assembled is configured to be validated the icon
is displayed indicating
that validation is needed; on the contrary if the icon is not displayed the Material Tracking Unit can be directly
assembled.
To validate the Material Tracking Unit, specify the values for Serial Number/Batch Id and Quantity, working
from the Operator Terminal page.
If the inserted code matches with the expected one, the
successful.

icon is displayed indicating that validation was

If the inserted code does not match with the expected one, the
icon is displayed indicating that validation
was not successful and that it is necessary to specify another Serial Number/Batch Id.
2. Click

Assemble.

Validating and Assembling Pre-kitted MTUs using Barcode Rules
1. If the Material Tracking Unit to be assembled has a Barcode Rule associated, the icon
indicating that a barcode has to be read.
If the inserted code matches with the expected one, the
successful.

is displayed

icon is displayed indicating that validation was

If the inserted code does not match with the expected one, the
icon is displayed indicating that validation
was not successful and that it is necessary to specify another Serial Number/Batch Id.
2. Click

Assemble.

11.3.2.4 How to Manage Quality Inspections
Quality Inspections are related to measurements to be collected at runtime in terms of frequency and sample size.
Measurements can be related to Attributive, Variable or Visual Quality Characteristics.
Depending on how you have configured the Quality Inspection, the form to be filled at runtime may be related to
the indication of:
the Values for Variable Characteristics.
Ok/Not Ok for Attributive Characteristics.
the number and the position of detected Defects in case of Visual Characteristics.

Accept or Reject for Buy-Off. If a Quality Inspection of type Buy-Off has been created, a Buy-Off notification can
be sent after the Work Order Operation has been started by clicking the

Send Buy-Off Notification button.

For Attributive and Variable Characteristics, you can associate only one Failure at a time.
For Visual Characteristics, you can associate multiple Failures at a time, however, it is not possible to
remove the association.
For Visual Characteristics, it is possible to click on the
Fullscreen button to see the visual in full
screen mode.
Quality Inspections specifically designed for Buy-Off only require the entitled user to Accept or Reject
the inspected MTUs and to insert the Electronic Signature. If the MTU is rejected, the Work Order
Operation or Work Order Step cannot be completed.
For Attributive and Variable Characteristics, you can take any number of measurements for each
sample, according to the sample size value that you have defined while configuring your Inspection
Definition. The UI will prompt you to complete all the required measurements.
For Visual Characteristics, you can take only one measurement for each sample (while configuring the
Inspection Definition, the value for the sample size is already set to 1).
If the Inspection Definition is associated to a Control Method, a graph with the specified Control Chart
will be shown and updated each time a sample is confirmed and the Subgroup Size is reached. For
more information on Control Methods, see How to Manage Control Methods in the Opcenter Execution
Foundation User Manual.
Information regarding the context in which the samples and values are taken (i.e. Work Order, Work
Order Operation, Inspection Order, Inspection Order Operation and Inspection Equipment) is saved in
the system so that statistical analysis can be performed with greater detail.
For Part Based, Time Based and Unit Based Quality Inspections, see Special Cases regarding Quality Inspections.

Prerequisites
An Inspection Definition is linked to the Inspection Order Operation.

Available Operations
Perform Quality Inspections
Associate Failures

Performing Quality Inspections
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Inspection Order Operation of interest.
Depending on how you have configured the Quality Inspection for that specific Work Order, the corresponding
form will be displayed in the details area.
2. If you have configured for Attributive Characteristics:
If there is no problem with the inspection value, click OK.
If a problem is observed, click NOK. To associate a Failure, follow the procedure below.
When you have taken all of the measurements that are required to complete the Quality Inspection, click
on the
button to confirm the sample.
3. If you have configured for Variable Characteristics, enter the inspection value in the Measured Value text box:
If the value lies between Nominal, Low and High parameters, a green bar appears inside the text box
which shows you how far the inserted value lies from the nominal value. Click on the

button to

confirm the value. In case you need to undo your changes, click on the
button. The inserted values
for each sample will be visible in the Value text box, and the Timestamp text box will contain the
indication of when the value has been confirmed.

If the value lies outside the range, a red bar is displayed inside the text box. To associate a Failure, follow
the procedure below.
If you need to edit some values, click on the

button and then click on the

button to confirm

the value. In case you need to undo your changes, click on the
button.
When you have taken all of the measurements that are required to complete the Quality Inspection, click
on the
button to confirm the sample.
4. If you have configured for Visual Characteristics:
If no Failure is detected, the No Defects detected toggle button is already switched on (
the sample by clicking the

button. This means the Sample is recorded with no Failures.

If a Failure is detected, click the
off (

). Confirm

No Defects detected toggle button. By clicking it, you will switch it

) and associate Failure as explained in the procedure below.

When you have associated all of the Failures, click on the
button to confirm the sample.
5. (Optional) If the Quality Inspection requires an Electronic Signature, click Sign. In the Signature Required
panel, provide the required signature and click Sign again. When the Electronic Signature is completed, the
button is enabled.
6. (Optional) If you need to acquire a new sample, click the
button. The data related to the Quality Inspection
previously executed will disappear, in order to insert the new data. The actions performed on the previous
Quality Inspection are recorded in the History tab of the Inspection Order Operation.

Associating Failures
1. Click
to open the Browse Failures panel, where all the potential Failures are displayed. Select the Failure
you need and then click Select.
2. If one of the potential Failures has sub-Failures, to associate one of it, click
Open on the Failure and then,
after selecting the sub-Failure you require, click Select.
3. (Optional) If you want to visualize all Failures present in the system and not only the potential ones, switch the
toggle button on (

). If you want to see only the potential Failures, switch the toggle button off (

).

(Only for Attributive and Variable) If you want to remove the association with the Failure, click the
x button contained inside the Failure chip.

11.3.2.4.1 Special Cases regarding Quality Inspections
Quality Inspections of type Part Based, Time Based or Unit Based follow specific rules and behave differently.

Part Based Quality Inspections
If you have configured your system in order to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection only on a subset of
produced items (Part Based), the scenario may vary, depending on various factors.
If desired, you can perform the Quality Inspection also on other pieces.
An
indicator will be displayed on the Quality Inspection to be performed and it will disappear once the Quality
Inspection has been successfully completed.
A value indicating the number of inspections to be executed is displayed as well.

Time Based Quality Inspections

If the system is configured to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection according to a specific interval of time
(Time Based), the first time you inspect a piece, a timer starts and the
icon is displayed on the Quality
Operation, to indicate that the timer has started and that for the moment no other Inspections are expected by the
system. However, you can decide to inspect other pieces, if necessary.
If the system is also configured to notify that the timer is approaching expiration, then you will see the
meaning that another may be performed.
If you inspect any piece, the timer is reset and you will see again the
expired, the icon changes to

icon,

icon. Otherwise, once the timer has

, indicating that the expected Quality Inspection is in delay.

Unit Based Quality Inspections
If the system is configured to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection according to a specific frequency of
produced items, expressed in the implicit Unit of Measure of the produced Material that is going to be inspected
(Unit Based), the scenario may vary, depending on various factors.
If desired, you can perform the Quality Inspection also on other pieces.
An
indicator will be displayed on the Quality Inspection to be performed and it will disappear once the Quality
Inspection has been successfully completed.
A value indicating the number of inspections to be executed is displayed as well.
By default, it is possible to move on in the production execution without performing any Quality Inspections. The
default behavior is governed by several pre-checks, which you can enable/disable from Solution Studio.
Pre-check

Disabled

Operation in the routing. It is
mandatory to perform at least one
Quality Inspection.

Operation in the routing, even if no
Quality Inspections whatsoever are
performed.

elapsed. It is mandatory to perform a
Quality Inspection again.

You cannot complete quantities on the
current Work Order Operation, if the
