completing the current Work Order
Operation.
Note This is the default behavior.

For more information on how to enable/disable prechecks in Solution Studio, see section Configuring Command
Settings of the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.

Optional Work Order Operations in the Routing
If the Work Order Operation containing the Quality Inspection in the routing is optional, then it will behave like a
standard optional Work Order Operation.

Work Order Operations containing only Quality Inspections
If, in a routing, the Work Order Operation dedicated to the Part Based and Unit Based Quality Inspection does not
contain other manufacturing activities to be performed, the pieces/Serial Numbers for which it is not required any
inspection are automatically skipped and become ready for the next Work Order Operation according to the
configured Frequency Value.
In case the Work Order Operation is also Out of Route, only pieces/Serial Numbers which require inspection
according to the configured Frequency Value becomes ready on it.
For Time Based, the Work Order Operation containing only the Quality Inspection well behave as an optional Work
Order Operation.

Work Order Operations containing various Manufacturing Activities
If the Work Order Operations contain various manufacturing activities in addition to the Quality Inspection, each
piece/Serial Number must be started and completed.

Work Orders modified after their generation from a Bill of Process
(For Part Based and Unit Based only) If several Work Orders are created from the same Bill of Process, they will
inherit the Part Based or Unit Based Quality Inspection configuration.
However, should the Referenced Operation be changed for one of the Quality Inspections pertaining to the "sibling"
Work Orders after being generated, the counter is no longer shared for that particular Quality Inspection and it is
triggered separately for each of the modified Work Order Operations.

Part Based or Unit Based Inspections with the same Main Manufacturing Operation
set as Referenced Operation
If a Part Based or Unit Based Inspection Definition is linked to an Operation and the same Operation is specified as
Referenced Operation, then the counter will count the pieces to be inspected as soon as they are started on the
corresponding Work Order Operation and no Material Tracking Unit is skipped.

11.3.2.5 Disassembling Material Tracking Units
It could be necessary to disassemble a fully-assembled Material Tracking Unit in the following cases:

A defective piece has been found on the fully-assembled MTU and the Production Coordinator decides to
disassemble (remove) this defective piece from the produced MTU.
The Production Coordinator decides that a partial Disassembly is necessary to remove a portion of the fullyassembled MTU and reserve it for future re-assembly.
It is also possible to disassemble a partially-assembled Material Tracking Unit before the corresponding Work Order
Operation for its assembly has been completed. For example, while assembling an MTU that must consist of three
colored pieces (red, white, green), the Operator discovers that he has erroneously assembled two red pieces and
one white one. To rectify this situation, one of the red pieces must be removed and replaced with a green piece:
therefore, the Operator must perform a partial Disassembly.
When performing a Disassembly that involves a Material Tracking Unit to which one or more Documents
have been associated and the quantity to be disassembled is less than the quantity of the assembled MTU,
the MTU will be split and the resulting MTU will be automatically associated to the same Documents that
were associated to the initial MTU.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is not in status Hold or Verified.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step of interest.
2. Click the Previous Data button: the list of MTUs that are currently assembled for the Work Order Operation or
Step in question is displayed.
3. Choose the consumed Material Tracking Unit of interest and click
Material will be automatically updated.

Disassemble. The quantity of the

The overall background color of the selected MTU, as well as the background color of its contained
elements, will change: for details, see page Consuming or Assembling Material Tracking Units.

11.3.2.6 Scrapping Consumed Material Tracking Units and Material Tracking
Units to be Consumed
During production, if a defective piece is detected on a previously assembled Material Tracking Unit or on Material
Tracking Unit that has not been assembled yet and the Operator decides to remove this defective piece from the
produced MTU, it is necessary to scrap the Material Tracking Unit.
In addition, in case of Batch MTUs, it is also possible to scrap their quantity partially.
The scrapped MTUs can be moved to a Container.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is not in status Hold, Verified or Completed.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation or Work Order Step of interest.
2. Click the Previous Data button: the list of MTUs that are currently assembled to the selected Work Order
Operation or Step is displayed.
3. Choose the consumed Material Tracking Unit of interest and click
Scrap.
4. (Optional) Select one or more Failures to be associated to the Material Tracking Unit to be scrapped.

5. (Only for To Be Consumed Material Tracking Units) Browse for the Material Tracking Unit or insert the Material
Tracking Unit code.
6. (Only for Batches) Enter the quantity to be scrapped.
7. (Optional) Browse for the Container or insert the Container code.
8. (Optional) Add a Comment.
9. Click Save.

#### 11.3.3 Adding Notes to Work Order Operations

Operators can write notes regarding the execution of a Work Order Operation: this could be useful to other
Operators. Also, Production Coordinators can add notes to provide extra information about a Work Order
Operation.
Notes can be added to a Work Order Operation, as well as to a Work Order. Notes that are added to a Work Order
Operation refer only to that specific Work Order Operation. Instead, when a Note is added to a Work Order, that
same Note will also be added to all the Work Order Operations making up that Work Order.
The notification must be properly configured, in order to be triggered after a Note has been added: this results in an
increment of the value displayed in the badge associated with the Notes. At this point, it is possible to handle the
note in question: for example, the person who receives the notification can then acknowledge the note.

Target User
Users with the Operator, as well as the Production Coordinator role, can perform this operation.

Prerequisite
The Work Order is released.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Notes button.
The Notes panel is displayed: any pre-existing notes that refer to the selected Work Order Operation will be
shown as tiles therein.
3. Depending on whether you want to add your note to an Operation or a Work Order, select either the Operation
or the Work Order tab.
4. In the New Note box, type the notes that you want to add.
5. Click

Save.

Results
The note that has just been added will be displayed as a tile on the panel itself corresponding to the selected
Operation or Work Order tab.
The value shown in the Notes badge will increase by 1.
Any notes that have been added will be visible inside the Notes tab of the As Built page corresponding
to the selected Work Order.
Added notes are displayed based on Sort By, Created On (latest date), or Status (acknowledged).

Acknowledging Notes

To acknowledge a note, click the
Acknowledge button. At this point, an
indicator will be displayed
inside the tile corresponding to the selected note and the badge value associated with the Notes will decrease by 1.

#### 11.3.4 Placing Work Orders on Hold

The status of the Work Order will be on hold. You need to close the Hold Reason in order to start or resume the Work
Order Operation.

Target User
Users with the Operator role can perform this operation.

Prerequisite
At least one Hold Reason has been configured.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select a Work Order Operation in the New, Hold, Pause or Queue status.
2. Click
Hold.
3. Select a Hold Reason for the Work Order.
4. Click OK.

#### 11.3.5 Pausing and Resuming Work Order Operations

If you are an Operator and issues in production arise, or changes need to be made to operations, you can pause a
Work Order that is currently in execution.
Pause By Different User
When the Pause/Complete Work Order Operation by Different User configuration key is enabled,
then the Work Order Operation can be paused by any User, even if the User did not start the Work
Order Operation.
When any Work Order Operation is Paused, then the machine involved in the execution of the Work
Order is automatically set from Busy to Ready for all Production types. In this case, as a prerequisite,
parameter Lockable parameter of the Equipment must be set to True.

Target User
Users with the Operator role can pause a Work Order Operation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select a Work Order Operation in the Active status.
2. Click
Pause.
3. Select a Reason for pausing the Work Order.
4. Click OK: if Electronic Signature is not enabled, this is sufficient to pause the Work Order. Otherwise, proceed to
the next step.
5. (Only if Electronic Signature has been enabled) In the prompt that appears on screen, type your password, some
notes and then click Validate User. The system checks your credentials against those of the list of authorized
Operators:
If you pass validation, you are authorized to complete pausing the Work Order.

If you fail validation, you are not authorized to complete pausing the Work Order, but you may continue
to handle Work Orders or Work Order Operations that do not have dependencies on the Work Order that
you are not authorized to pause.

Result
The system sets the Work Order status to Pause: if you want to resume the Work Order Operation, click

Start.

Any Equipment initially locked by the Work Order Operation will be released after the Operation has been
paused.

#### 11.3.6 Adding Documents at Runtime

In addition to those added during the creation of a Work Order, Documents can be added to Work Order Operations
at runtime:
by users with the Operator role, during the execution of a Work Order.
by users with the Production Coordinator role, for present or past Work Orders.

Prerequisite
The Collect Document parameter has been set while creating the Work Order Operation.
The status of the Work Order Operations is Active.

Procedure for users with Operator role
1. In the Operator Terminal page , select a Work Order Operation.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Add Documents button: this opens the Add Documents page,
which displays a navigation wizard on the far left.
3. Click the Add Documents step of the wizard.
4. Do either of the following:
Search for documents already existing in the system and select one or more documents: a badge for
each Document selected is displayed next to the navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected
Documents area.
Click
, to browse the document not yet available in the system and then import it. For tablets and
mobile devices, using this button, it is also possible to take a picture of the Failure involved and then
import it.
5. (Optional and only if the Work Order is of type Serialized) Click the Select Material Tracking Units step of the
wizard and select one or more tiles representing the Material Tracking Units to be associated with the
document: these tiles are displayed directly under the search filter provided.
6. In the Summary section, review the information and click Submit.

Procedure for users with Production Coordinator role
1. Click the

Work Orders tile in the home page.

2. Select the Work Order of interest and click
As Built Order.
3. Click the Operations tab, and select the Work Order Operation to which you want to add documents.
4. (Optional, only for Serialized Work Orders) If you want to add documents to a specific Serial Number, select it in
the Serial Numbers tab.
5. Click the Collected Documents tab.
6. Click

Add.

7. On the Add Documents page, do either:
If a document you require is available in the list, select it, and click Add.
If a document is not available, click Import, and using the file explorer, select the document. On the Add
Documents page, update the Identifier if required, and click Save.

#### 11.3.7 How to Manage Non-Productive Activities

Non-Productive Activities include for example workspace cleaning, tool maintenance and training, and can be
managed directly from the Operator Terminal while managing Work Order Operations. In details:
Multiple Activities can be started or completed at the same time.
It is possible to start and/or complete Activities belonging only to the currently-logged User.
Start and/or end dates of the Activities are automatically set to the current time.
Activities cannot be deleted from the log.
In addition, Production Coordinators and Product Engineers can manage Non-Productive Activities of all Users from
the Users page.

Starting Non-Productive Activities
1. In the Operator Terminal page, click
N-P Activities on the vertical command bar. The Assigned NonProductive Activities panel displays the activities you have previously started.
2. Click Assign and Start.
3. In the Assign Non-Productive Activities panel, select one or more activities among those previously created.
4. (Optional) In the Context drop down list, select the context of the activity.
5. If you have selected a context, insert the context items according to option selected in the previous step.
6. Click Save.

Completing Non-Productive Activities
1. In the Operator Terminal page, click
N-P Activities on the vertical command bar. The Assigned NonProductive Activities page displays the activities you have previously started.
2. Select one or more activities and click Complete. The selected activities are logged as completed with the
current date and time.

#### 11.3.8 Closing Flexible Work Orders

During production execution, it may be necessary to close a Flexible Work Order before its full completion. This may
happen, for example if the Work Order had been created for a given work shift and consequently it is necessary to
close it until the end of that shift, even if some Work Order Operations have not yet been completed.
When a Flexible Work Order is closed, if there are Work Order Operations that need to be completed, they will be
closed together with the Work Order: in this case, a copy of the original Work Order is created to permit the
completion of those Work Order Operations that were still in progress, in order to produce the remainder of the
original Work Order.

Prerequisites
All the Work Order Operations of the Work Order need to be executed at least once.
The Work Order status is either Queue or Pause.
The Production Type of the Work Order is FlexibleBatch or FlexibleSerialized.

Target User

Users with the Production Coordinator role or the Operator role can perform this operation.

Procedure
1. Do either of the following:
In the Work Orders page, select the Work Order to be closed.
In the Operator Terminal or Operator Terminal details page, select one of the Work Order Operation
the Work Order to be closed consists of.
2. Click
Close Flexible Work Order.
3. Either insert the unique identifier of the Work Order in the edit box or click the Generate button to
automatically create it according to a predefined Numbering Pattern.
4. Click Save.

Result
The status of the Work Order changes to Complete.
Work Order Operations in status Open are set to NotExecuted, while Work Order Operations in status Partial or
Complete do not change.
The Work Order quantity is automatically adjusted by the system and you can no longer modify it.
A new Work Order related to the closed one is created as follows:
with Production Type TransferBatch, if the original Work Order was of type FlexibleBatch
with Production Type Serialized, if the original Work Order was of type FlexibleSerialized.
The new Work Order contains a copy of the Work Order Operations contained in the closed one, and their status
correspond to the status of the original Work Order Operations at the moment of the closure.
The not produced quantity is assigned to the new Work Order.
All completed and scrapped items are logged for the closed Work Order both in the As Built Report and in the
Genealogy page.
All in progress and not started items are logged for the closed Work Order both in the As Built Report and in the
Genealogy page.

#### 11.3.9 Scrapping Serial Numbers or Quantities from Work Order Operations and Work Order Steps

After releasing a Work Order, if an unexpected event occurs, it is possible to scrap one or more Serial Numbers from
Work Order Operations or Work Order Steps, thereby permitting its completion, even though less quantity is
produced than expected.

Target User
Users with the Production Coordinator role or the Operator role can perform this operation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal page, select a Work Order Operation or Work Order Step and then click the
Scrap
button.
2. (Optional) Select one or more Failures to be associated to the quantities to be scrapped.
3. Select at least one Serial Number to be scrapped (only one Serial Number is available is a Work Order Step is
selected).
4. (Optional) Insert a comment.
5. Click Save.

### 11.4 Completing Work Order Operations

When executing Work Orders, you can complete Work Order Operations which have been previously started and
managed.
A Work Order is to be considered as completed when all its Work Order Operations are in a final status (either
the Complete or the Not Executed status).
During the creation of the Work Order to which the Work Order Operation to be completed belongs, it is possible to
define a set of Outbound Interlocking Checks to verify various conditions (for example, the assembly of all required
Materials, the completion of the related Steps). If at least one active Outbound Interlocking Check fails, the Work
Order Operation/Step will not be completed: an error message for each of the failed Outbound Interlocking Checks
is displayed to the Operator.

Constraints involving Work Order Steps
Completing Work Order Steps on Machines is not feasible if the Machines are in one of the following statuses:
OnHold
Repaired
Scrapped.
This behavior of the system is governed by the following pre-checks, which are enabled by default:
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnStartWOStepFullQty
CheckEquipmentHoldOnStartWOStepFullQty
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnCompleteWOStepFullQty
CheckEquipmentHoldOnCompleteWOStepFullQty
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnStartWOStepSerialized
CheckEquipmentHoldOnStartWOStepSerialized
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnCompleteWOStepSerialized
CheckEquipmentHoldOnCompleteWOStepSerialized
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnStartWOStepTransferBatch
CheckEquipmentHoldOnStartWOStepTransferBatch
CheckMachineNonConformanceOnCompleteWOStepTransferBatch
CheckEquipmentHoldOnCompleteWOStepTransferBatch
You can disable these pre-checks in Solution Studio as explained in section Configuring Command Settings of
the Opcenter Execution Foundation Development and Configuration Guide.

Procedure
Work Order Operations can be completed also inside the Operator Terminal details page by clicking
Complete on the vertical command bar. Keep in mind that, for Serialized and FlexibleSerialized Work
Order Operations completed inside the Operator Terminal details page, you can complete only one Serial
Number at a time.
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation to be completed.
2. If more than one Serial Number is present for the Work Order Operation belonging to Serialized or Flexible
Serialized production type, do either of the following:
select the tiles representing the Serial Numbers (MTUs) that can be completed for the selected Work
Order Operation.
select the
3. Click

Select / Deselect All check box, to include all the listed Serial Numbers.

Complete.

4. (Optional) If your Production Coordinator selected Electronic Signature Complete creating the Work Order
Operation, you are prompted to type your password, some notes and then to click Validate User.

### 11.5 Declaring Quality Non-Conformances during Production

If a Failure was detected during production, you must open a Quality Non-Conformance.
Based on the settings of the Allow Opening a Non-Conformance on Parent Failure Configuration Key,
Non-Conformances can be created on Failures and their respective Sub-Failures in the hierarchy.
While creating Non-Conformances, Failures can be filtered with respect to Material or Work Order
Operation based on the Configuration Key Runtime Failure Association Management settings.

Prerequisite
The involved Work Order Operation is in status Open or Active.
The involved Failure is in status Current.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Work Order Operation of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Declare NC button: this opens the Create Non-Conformance
page, which displays a navigation wizard on the left.
3. Select the tile(s) representing the Failure(s) involved in the Quality Non-Conformance that you want to open: the
Add Defect step of the wizard turns green and a badge for each Failure selected is displayed next to the
navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected Defects area.
4. Click the Add Details step of the wizard and then click one of the following tabs to select the context in which
the Failure has been encountered:
Work Order Operation
Material Tracking Unit
Equipment
Tool
5. Set the following parameters:
Parameter

Description

Identifier

Unique identifier to be assigned to the Non-Conformance. Once saved, this parameter can no
longer be modified.

Severity

The severity for the Failure. Possible values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

Comments

(Optional) Any additional information regarding the Failure observed.

6. Select one or more tiles representing the items involved in the Failure.
Only for Material Tracking Unit tab
This filter makes it possible to conduct searches on MTUs by Material Tracking Unit Code or Material
NId: by default, the Show only Material Tracking Units related to the selected Work Order
Operation toggle is set to true, thus allowing you to make your selections from among solely those

MTUs related to the selected Work Order Operation. If you want to make a selection from among all
MTUs (including those not related to the selected Work Order Operation), set this toggle to false so that
all MTUs are displayed for selection.
7. (Optional) Click the Add Documents step of the wizard. In the Documents tab, you can select the tile(s)
representing the Document(s) involved in the Quality Non-Conformance that you want to open: a badge for
each Document selected is displayed next to the navigation wizard, as well as in the Selected Documents area.
If the Document you want to add is not already in the list, do either of the following:
click

, to attach a document already present in the system.

click
, to browse the document of your choice and then import it. For tablets and mobile devices,
using this button, it is also possible to take a picture of the Failure involved and then import it.
8. In the Summary section, review the information and click Submit.

Results
A Non-Conformance is created in status Open.
The state of the last Work Order Operation in the routing changes from Open to Future Hold: the state indicator
changes to
. If the last Operation in the routing is in a state different from Open, its state changes directly
to Hold.
The nested card's background color used to represent the status of the activity changes to pale red.

### 11.6 Requesting Change Packages on Work Order Operations

At runtime, the Operator can notify the Production Coordinator that some changes must be made on-the-fly to the
Work Order Operation or Step (in particular, to the set of Specifications required to successfully execute that
specific Work Order Operation or Step). This is known as a Change Package request.

Prerequisites
Only one Work Order Operation has been selected and it belongs to the TransferBatch production type.
The involved Work Order Operation or Step is in status Open or Partial.
No other Change Packages have been requested for the involved Work Order Operation or Step.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, or Operator Terminal details select the Work Order Operation or Step (only if you
are operating inside the Operator Terminal details) of interest.
2. On the vertical command bar, click the
Change Package button: a dedicated panel opens, where you can
insert information on the changes to be made to the Work Order Operation or Step.
3. Click Save. Once the Change Package has been saved, it can no longer be updated.

Results
A Change Package is created in status Editing.
The status of the involved Work Order Operation is changed to Hold: the status indicator changes to

.

Likewise, the
indicator is displayed only on the Work Order Operation (even if the Change Package was
created for the Work Order Step), indicating that a Change Package request has been opened either on the Work
Order Operation or one of its Steps.
On the Vertical Command Bar and in the Nested-Card Zone, the
buttons are disabled.

Start,

Pause and

Complete

On the involved Work Order Operation, no actions are allowed (for example, the adding of Materials, Documents
or Tools). The same behavior applies also to the related Steps.
It is not possible to open Non-Conformances of type Change on the involved Work Order Operation.

## 12 How to Execute Inspection Orders in the Operator Terminal for Quality Execution

The Operator Terminal for Quality Execution is recommended for quality-testing activities.

Main Workflow
Display Document Files
Manage Inspection Order Operations
Perform Quality Inspections

### 12.1 Navigation of the Operator Terminal for Quality Execution

The Operator Terminal is the main environment in which you will move to carry out quality execution. It is from
here that you will be able to view and select your Inspection Order Operations.
Here is what you need to know to navigate this environment and interpret what is displayed on screen, as well as
adapt its contents according to your needs.

Prerequisite
A piece of Equipment has been selected and associated to a particular terminal, as explained on page Selecting the
Equipment of the Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation Guide.

Indicators Visible on Tiles
The tiles presented in the Operator Terminal may contain dedicated indicators that allow you to interpret the
current status of an Inspection Order Operation displayed in tile format.
Consult the legend below to comprehend their meaning:

Indicator

Meaning
Ready
Active
Complete

Accessing the Working Environment
1. In the Operator Terminal page, do one of the following:
In the Equipment box on the left, type the Equipment ID (or part of it): the system displays all the
Inspection Order Operations that match your search criteria and can be executed on that Equipment.
In the Material box in the middle, type the ID of the Material: the system displays all the Inspection Order
Operations that match your search criteria and can be executed.
In the Inspection Order MTU Code box on the right, type the Code of the Material Tracking Unit: the
system displays all the Inspection Order Operations that match your search criteria and can be executed.
2. Select one or more Inspection Order Operations of interest and click the
Open icon: this opens the
Operator Terminal details page from which you can see the details of the related Runtime Inspection
Definitions to be executed for the selected Inspection Order Operations.

### 12.2 Displaying Document Files

The Document Viewer makes it possible for users to view any Documents that have been attached to an Inspection
Order Operation.
Document visualization depends on its file format: see Supported File Formats.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Inspection Order Operation for which you want to view its attached
Document.
2. Operating on the Vertical Command Bar, click the
Documents button to hide or unhide the Document
Preview. The lower area displays the list of the documents associated to the current Inspection Order Operation.
3. Click the preview or the

icon to expand the document to full screen. Click

to switch to the list mode

(here you can find the size of the document and also download the document by clicking

).

Supported File Formats
The Document Viewer supports the following file formats:

File Types

Supported File Formats

Non-video files with preview

.txt/.html
.svg
.jpg
.png
.pdf

Video files with preview

.mp4
.ogg
.webm

Other file formats, such as:
.doc
.csv
.xml
.zip
are not displayed by the Document Viewer, but can be downloaded.

### 12.3 Managing Inspection Order Operations

In the Operator Terminal, when executing Inspection Orders, you can perform various activities on an Inspection
Order Operation.

Environment Description

Inspection Operation List - On the left of the screen. Displays the list of the selected Inspection Order
Operations.

Nested-Card Zone - In the center of the screen, immediately after the Inspection Operation List. Displays the
elements needed to perform the current activity.
Document Viewer - On the right of the screen, immediately after the nested-card zone. Upon clicking the
Documents button on the vertical command bar, the Document Viewer displays the Documents associated to
the current Inspection Order Operation.
Vertical Command Bar - On the far right of the screen. Displays a set of buttons to perform various activities on
the current Inspection Order Operation.

Operations Available in the Inspection Operation List
Displays the Inspection Order Operations selected in the Operator Terminal page.
In addition, you can also:
click the
status.

Show All Operations icon (default) to display all Inspection Order Operations, regardless of their

click the
Hide Completed Operations icon to display only those Inspection Order Operations that have
not yet been completed (that is, their status differs from Completed).

Operations Available in the Nested-Card Zone
The content of the nested-card zone depends on the activities to be performed on the current Inspection Order
Operation. The Quality Inspections to be performed on the Inspection Order Operation will be executed on the MTU
code indicated in the related input box. If the MTU code is not indicated, the Quality Inspection is performed on the
MTU code inserted during the creation of the Inspection Order. If no MTU code is indicated at all, the Quality
Inspection is performed on the Material that has the matching Part Number.

Operations Available in the Vertical Command Bar
According to their prerequisites, some buttons might be disabled.
Click the
Documents button to view the Documents attached to the selected Inspection Order Operation in
the Document Viewer zone.

What to do next
After completing the Runtime Inspection Definitions, you can complete the related Inspection Order Operations in
the Inspection Order page.

#### 12.3.1 How to Manage Quality Inspections

Quality Inspections are related to measurements to be collected at runtime in terms of frequency and sample size.
Measurements can be related to Attributive, Variable or Visual Quality Characteristics.
Depending on how you have configured the Quality Inspection, the form to be filled at runtime may be related to
the indication of:
the Values for Variable Characteristics.
Ok/Not Ok for Attributive Characteristics.
the number and the position of detected Defects in case of Visual Characteristics.
Accept or Reject for Buy-Off. If a Quality Inspection of type Buy-Off has been created, a Buy-Off notification can
be sent after the Work Order Operation has been started by clicking the

Send Buy-Off Notification button.

For Attributive and Variable Characteristics, you can associate only one Failure at a time.
For Visual Characteristics, you can associate multiple Failures at a time, however, it is not possible to
remove the association.
For Visual Characteristics, it is possible to click on the
Fullscreen button to see the visual in full
screen mode.
Quality Inspections specifically designed for Buy-Off only require the entitled user to Accept or Reject
the inspected MTUs and to insert the Electronic Signature. If the MTU is rejected, the Work Order
Operation or Work Order Step cannot be completed.
For Attributive and Variable Characteristics, you can take any number of measurements for each
sample, according to the sample size value that you have defined while configuring your Inspection
Definition. The UI will prompt you to complete all the required measurements.
For Visual Characteristics, you can take only one measurement for each sample (while configuring the
Inspection Definition, the value for the sample size is already set to 1).
If the Inspection Definition is associated to a Control Method, a graph with the specified Control Chart
will be shown and updated each time a sample is confirmed and the Subgroup Size is reached. For
more information on Control Methods, see How to Manage Control Methods in the Opcenter Execution
Foundation User Manual.
Information regarding the context in which the samples and values are taken (i.e. Work Order, Work
Order Operation, Inspection Order, Inspection Order Operation and Inspection Equipment) is saved in
the system so that statistical analysis can be performed with greater detail.
For Part Based, Time Based and Unit Based Quality Inspections, see Special Cases regarding Quality Inspections.

Prerequisites
An Inspection Definition is linked to the Inspection Order Operation.

Available Operations
Perform Quality Inspections
Associate Failures

Performing Quality Inspections
1. In the Operator Terminal, select the Inspection Order Operation of interest.
Depending on how you have configured the Quality Inspection for that specific Work Order, the corresponding
form will be displayed in the details area.
2. If you have configured for Attributive Characteristics:
If there is no problem with the inspection value, click OK.
If a problem is observed, click NOK. To associate a Failure, follow the procedure below.
When you have taken all of the measurements that are required to complete the Quality Inspection, click
on the
button to confirm the sample.
3. If you have configured for Variable Characteristics, enter the inspection value in the Measured Value text box:
If the value lies between Nominal, Low and High parameters, a green bar appears inside the text box
which shows you how far the inserted value lies from the nominal value. Click on the

button to

confirm the value. In case you need to undo your changes, click on the
button. The inserted values
for each sample will be visible in the Value text box, and the Timestamp text box will contain the
indication of when the value has been confirmed.
If the value lies outside the range, a red bar is displayed inside the text box. To associate a Failure, follow
the procedure below.

If you need to edit some values, click on the

button and then click on the

button to confirm

the value. In case you need to undo your changes, click on the
button.
When you have taken all of the measurements that are required to complete the Quality Inspection, click
on the
button to confirm the sample.
4. If you have configured for Visual Characteristics:
If no Failure is detected, the No Defects detected toggle button is already switched on (
the sample by clicking the

button. This means the Sample is recorded with no Failures.

If a Failure is detected, click the
off (

). Confirm

No Defects detected toggle button. By clicking it, you will switch it

) and associate Failure as explained in the procedure below.

When you have associated all of the Failures, click on the
button to confirm the sample.
5. (Optional) If the Quality Inspection requires an Electronic Signature, click Sign. In the Signature Required
panel, provide the required signature and click Sign again. When the Electronic Signature is completed, the
button is enabled.
6. (Optional) If you need to acquire a new sample, click the
button. The data related to the Quality Inspection
previously executed will disappear, in order to insert the new data. The actions performed on the previous
Quality Inspection are recorded in the History tab of the Inspection Order Operation.

Associating Failures
1. Click
to open the Browse Failures panel, where all the potential Failures are displayed. Select the Failure
you need and then click Select.
2. If one of the potential Failures has sub-Failures, to associate one of it, click
Open on the Failure and then,
after selecting the sub-Failure you require, click Select.
3. (Optional) If you want to visualize all Failures present in the system and not only the potential ones, switch the
toggle button on (

). If you want to see only the potential Failures, switch the toggle button off (

).

(Only for Attributive and Variable) If you want to remove the association with the Failure, click the
x button contained inside the Failure chip.

12.3.1.1 Special Cases regarding Quality Inspections
Quality Inspections of type Part Based, Time Based or Unit Based follow specific rules and behave differently.

Part Based Quality Inspections
If you have configured your system in order to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection only on a subset of
produced items (Part Based), the scenario may vary, depending on various factors.
If desired, you can perform the Quality Inspection also on other pieces.
An
indicator will be displayed on the Quality Inspection to be performed and it will disappear once the Quality
Inspection has been successfully completed.
A value indicating the number of inspections to be executed is displayed as well.

Time Based Quality Inspections
If the system is configured to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection according to a specific interval of time
(Time Based), the first time you inspect a piece, a timer starts and the

icon is displayed on the Quality

Operation, to indicate that the timer has started and that for the moment no other Inspections are expected by the
system. However, you can decide to inspect other pieces, if necessary.
If the system is also configured to notify that the timer is approaching expiration, then you will see the
meaning that another may be performed.
If you inspect any piece, the timer is reset and you will see again the
expired, the icon changes to

icon,

icon. Otherwise, once the timer has

, indicating that the expected Quality Inspection is in delay.

Unit Based Quality Inspections
If the system is configured to automatically trigger a Quality Inspection according to a specific frequency of
produced items, expressed in the implicit Unit of Measure of the produced Material that is going to be inspected
(Unit Based), the scenario may vary, depending on various factors.
If desired, you can perform the Quality Inspection also on other pieces.
An
indicator will be displayed on the Quality Inspection to be performed and it will disappear once the Quality
Inspection has been successfully completed.
A value indicating the number of inspections to be executed is displayed as well.
By default, it is possible to move on in the production execution without performing any Quality Inspections. The
default behavior is governed by several pre-checks, which you can enable/disable from Solution Studio.

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Navigation of the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow

13 How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One
Piece Flow
The Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow is recommended for high-automated production.

Main Workflow
Preview Document Files
Declare Milestones
Manage Traceability Operations
Manage Defects
Manage Generic Data Collection Operations
Manage Screwing Operations
Manage Open Protocol Operations
Manage Mandatory Positive Confirmation

### 13.1 Navigation of the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow

Here below is what you need to know to navigate the Operator Terminal and interpret what is displayed on screen,
as well as adapt its contents according to your needs.
When the Material Tracking Unit enters the Equipment:
all Operations to be executed are automatically started and their status is changed to Active.
Milestones associated with the Equipment are checked.
If a takt time has been configured for the Equipment, a dedicated counter indicates the countdown (in seconds)
from when the Production Item enters the Equipment until the configured takt time has elapsed. The counter is:
green, when the takt time is still far from elapsing.
yellow, if the takt time is approaching its elapsing.
red, if the takt time has elapsed.
If the Operations have different sequence numbers (for example: 10, 20, 30), then only those Operations with the
lowest sequence number are visible in the screen. In the Operations page, the sequence numbers of the
Operations are visible in ascending order and, clicking on the number of your choice, it is possible to filter the
operations under the related tabs. If all the Operations have the same sequence number, the related chip is not
visible.
When the Material Tracking Unit exits the Equipment:
Operations correctly executed change their status to Complete.
Operations not executed remain in Partial status, with Operation not Executed or Activity not Executed
result, according to the Result management behavior.
Operations not yet executed or with a negative (NOK) result are then visible in the Quality Gate page, where
they can be repaired or completed.
the quantity of the Materials contained in the Line Side Position associated with the Equipment is automatically
decreased, triggering a Kanban call when the threshold reaches or falls below the configured value.

Prerequisite
A piece of Equipment has been selected and associated to a particular terminal, as explained on page Selecting the
Equipment of the Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation Guide.

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Navigation of the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow

remain in the Workplace.

4. Click Save.

1. To access the Operator Terminal, log in with your credentials as an Operator.
2. In the Header Bar, insert the Identifier of the MTU of interest.
3. Click either Enter or the

button: the MTU has entered the Equipment.

To exit the Equipment, click the

button.

Previewing Document Files

### 13.2 Previewing Document Files

The Document Viewer makes it possible for users to preview any Documents that have been attached to a
Workplace and to the Work Order Operations, and to their related entities.
For Quality Gates, it is possible to preview the Documents attached to the Operations that must be repaired, and to
their related entities.
Document visualization depends on its file format: see Supported File Formats.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, click either the Operations page, to see the documents attached to the Work Order
Operation(s), or the Quality Gate page, to see the documents attached to the Work Order Operation(s) to be
repaired.
2. The lower right area of the screen displays the list of available documents, grouped by the entities to which they
are associated. Only the groups that contains documents are displayed. Select the desired group according to
the following list:
Operation for the documents associated to the current Work Order Operation.
Runtime for the documents associated to the Serial Numbers started by the current Work Order
Operation.
Product for:
the documents associated to the Final Material produced by the current Work Order Operation.

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Declaring Milestones

the documents associated to the active Material Tracking Units produced by the current Work
Order Operation.
Parts for the documents associated to the Materials consumed by the current Work Order Operation.
Tools for the documents associated to the Tools used by the current Work Order Operation.
Machines for the documents associated to the Machines used by the current Work Order Operation.
3. (Optional) Click the preview or the

icon to expand the document to full screen. Click

to switch to list

mode (here you can find the size of the document and also download the document by clicking

).

Supported File Formats
The following file formats for Documents are supported:
File Types

Supported File Formats

Non-video files with preview

.txt/.html
.svg
.jpg
.png
.pdf

Video files with preview

.mp4
.ogg
.webm

Other file formats, such as:
.doc
.csv
.xml
.zip
are not displayed by the Document Viewer, but can be downloaded.

### 13.3 Declaring Milestones

In the Milestone Declaration page, the Operator can:
view the list of Milestones for that Equipment, and see those reachable and those not reachable.
declare a Milestone for the Material Tracking Unit, performing the corresponding Transitions.
view the declared Milestone in the Milestone Activity History section.

Prerequisites
The root level of the Equipment Hierarchy must be Site.
A Production Flow Control Diagram has been properly configured.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, click
to enter the Milestone Declaration page. The system displays which
Milestones are reachable and which are not reachable (due to certain conditions not being satisfied). The
following information is displayed within each Milestone tile:

the source one, it cannot create a new MTU.

the transition between the source Milestone and the target Milestone has been
created beforehand in the corresponding Diagram.

This parameter is displayed only if you have inserted a new MTU Identifier (for
example, because the MTU enters the first Equipment of the plant). To automatically
create the MTU, declare first a Milestone associated with the INITIAL Attribute.

The MTU cannot enter the Milestone, because at least one of the preliminary Checks has failed.

When the MTU enters the Equipment to which Traceability Operations are linked, if the Operator insert a Barcode,
the system automatically compares the received Barcode with the configured Barcode Rule and behaves

There are other cases in which an Operation Result can be negative:
the Operation/Activity has never been traced: NOK Result, with Reason Operation not executed in case of
Operations; Activity not executed in case of Activities.
the Material has been configured as Serialized and the traced Serial Number has already been used before. In
this case, Work Order Operations are started but not completed, even if the part number is correct: NOK Result,
with Reason Already Used.
the Operator bypasses a Traceability Operation, because the required part is not available (for example, a
Bumper in case of an Operation which requires its installation). Bypassed Operations cannot be executed
anymore on that Equipment and must be completed in another Equipment or in a Quality Gate. They remain in
Partial status with a NOK Result, Reason Bypassed.
no Materials are associated to the Operations: they are already displayed in red (
NOK status) and the
Operator cannot perform any action. NOK Result, with Reason Material Not Available.
the traced Material has been configured as a Batch whose maximum quantity has been exceeded. NOK
Result, with Reason Batch quantity exceeded.

Prerequisites
Barcode Rules have been properly configured.
A Master Plan has been created with at least one Process Operation associated to a Material specification.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, click
to access the Operations page. The Material Traceability
Operations area displays the linked Traceability Operations.
2. Insert the Barcode and click Enter. According to the part number verification, the system automatically displays
three different results:
in case of no matches, an error message is displayed and Work Order Operations are not executed.
if the part number verification fails, Work Order Operations are not completed, not checked (

) and

if the part number verification succeeds, Work Order Operations are completed, checked (

OK status). The traced part number is displayed under the Operation.

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Managing Defects

Example
For a Traceability operation called Trace Steering Wheel, different cases are possible:
a Barcode for a different item, for example a Door, is scanned: no matches are found and consequently no
operations are executed.
a Barcode for the wrong steering wheel is scanned: the Barcode matches, but the part verification fails and
operations are not completed.
a Barcode for the correct steering wheel is scanned: both Barcode and part verification match and operations
are completed.
a Barcode for the correct steering wheel is scanned: both Barcode and part verification match, but the
uniqueness check fails and operations are not completed.

### 13.5 Managing Defects

At runtime, the Operator can browse the Failures hierarchy properly configured during the engineering phase, to
create a new Defect.
Starting from a specific category of root Failures (for example: Door Scratch), it is possible to quickly add a Defect in
Open state.
The visible root Failures are those associated with the part of the plant in which the Operator Terminal is located or
those that are not associated to any specific part of the plant.
If the Operator realizes that the Defect has been opened by mistake, it can be closed, so that its state changes
to Cancelled.
In addition, the Operator can:
increase or decrease the amount of Defects, automatically updating the related Counter
add or update Notes, containing information on a specific Defect.
The total amount of Defects opened for a specific piece of Equipment is displayed in the right sidebar, next to the
button.

Prerequisites
The Failure hierarchy has been properly configured.
An MTU has been created in one of the following ways:
manually
through a Milestone associated with the INITIAL Attribute
as a Serial Number of a Work Order.

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Managing Generic Data Collection Operations

Defects button in the right sidebar.
2. In the Defects pane, in the Open Defects accordion, click:
to decrease the amount of open Defects for each Sub-Failure
to increase the amount of open Defects for each Sub-Failure
to insert or update, in case of changes, additional information on the Defect.

accordion:

.

if the Root Failure has Sub-Failures, click the tile corresponding to the root Failure and click
one of the Sub-Failures displayed, according to your needs, and click
Defect appears in the Open Defects accordion highlighted in blue.

### 13.7 Managing Screwing Operations

When the MTU enters the Equipment to which Screwing operations are linked, the Operator can view the related
operations along with their result.
Each Operation displays the amount of completed rundowns, as well as the values acquired from the field. These
values are automatically updated each time the Automation Layer communicates with the system, updating the
operation result as well.

### 13.8 Managing Open Protocol Operations

The Open Protocol Operations widget replaces the Screwing Operations widget only if the Open Protocol
Service asset has been properly configured.
When the MTU enters the Equipment to which Open Protocol Operations are linked, the Operator can view the
related operations along with their result.
Each Operation displays the amount of completed rundowns, as well as the values acquired from the field. These
values are automatically updated each time the Open Protocol Tool communicates with the Open Protocol Node,
updating the operation result as well.

The Open Protocol Service asset has been properly configured.
A Tool with Logistic Class Open Protocol has been associated to the Process Operation.

### 13.9 Managing Mandatory Positive Confirmation

In certain cases, before the MTU exits the Equipment, the Operator must perform a manual verification to declare
the result of those Operations for which a Mandatory Positive Confirmation has been set.

Prerequisite
A Master Plan has been created with at least one Process Operation, with Operation Category of type Mandatory
Positive Confirmation.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, click
to access the Operations page.
2. In the Mandatory Positive Confirmation section, the list of Operations for which a mandatory positive
confirmation is required are displayed. For each Operation you can perform the following operations:
click

for Work Order Operations which are completed. As a result of this action, they are

automatically displayed in green and checked (
click

OK status).

for Work Order Operations which have not been executed/completed. As a result of this

action, they are automatically displayed in red and not checked (

NOK status).

### 13.10 How to Manage Quality Gates

In the Equipment that has been declared as Quality Gate, the Operator can view unresolved issues and perform
corrective actions, on the monitored Equipment and its nested child Equipment.
Issues can be:
active Defects.
Work Order Operations not yet executed or with a negative (NOK) result.
In the Quality Gate, Defects can be:
declared as not an issue (for example, if opened by mistake).
re-opened, if the issue has not been fixed.
fixed (and therefore closed).
certified.
Work Order Operations that are still to be managed can be executed (or re-executed) in case of negative results
(when applicable), so that they can be successfully completed.
To perform the necessary corrective actions, different UI accordions are displayed in the page, each provided with a
counter indicating the amount of unresolved issues.

Accessing the Working Environment
To access the Quality Gate page, click

in the Operator Terminal.

Prerequisite
The Equipment has been configured as Quality Gate.

Available Operations
Manage Active Defects
Amend negative (NOK) or still to be managed results for:

Material Traceability
Mandatory Positive Confirmation

Screwing Operations
Open Protocol Operations Note: if the Open Protocol Service asset has been configured, the Open
Protocol Operations accordion replaces the Screwing Operations accordion.

Managing Active Defects
1. In the Defects accordion, the list of active Defects is displayed. According to the action to be performed on the
Defect of interest, click on the different buttons listed below:
Action

Button

Not found
Fix
Certify
2. (Optional) If necessary, click the
button to add or view existing Notes.
3. (Optional) Repeat step 1 for each Defect whose status must be changed.

Amending Traceability Operations
A Material has been associated to the Operation during the engineering phase, otherwise the Operation is
not managed by the Quality Gate.
1. Click the Material Traceability Operations accordion.
2. Click the
button next to the Operation of interest. The Insert Barcode window is displayed.
3. Insert the Barcode and click Enter. According to the part number verification, the corresponding results are
displayed.
4. (Optional) Repeat steps 2-3 for each Operation that must be amended.

Amending Mandatory Positive Confirmations
1. Click the Mandatory Positive Confirmation accordion.
2. Click on the Operation of interest and perform the manual confirmation as required.
3. (Optional) Repeat step 2 for each Operation that must be amended.

Amending Generic Data Collection Operations
1. Click the Data Collection Operations accordion.
2. Select a Data Collection Operation and click
.
3. Click Re-Edit, to modify the values for the Data Collection sections (and steps) displayed.
4. After you have entered all required values, click Confirm and then Close.
5. (Optional) Repeat steps 2-4 for each Operation that must be amended.

Amending Screwing Operations

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Managing Line Side Positions

1. Click the Screwing Operations accordion.
2. Select a Screwing Operation and perform either of the following:
click

if the Operation has been completed, or if the Operator decides that it can be repaired

(Reason: Operator Backup). The Operation is automatically displayed in green and checked (
OK status).
click

if the Operation has not been executed, or if the Operator decides that it cannot be repaired

(Reason: Operation Failed). The Operation is automatically displayed in red and not checked (
NOK status).
3. (Optional) If necessary, click
in the Operation's tile to access a dedicated pane, containing details on the
Operation, such as: values provided for bolts, or related to the screwing torque and angle applied.
4. (Optional) Repeat steps 2-3 for each Operation that must be amended.

Amending Open Protocol Operations
This accordion is visible only if the Open Protocol Service asset has been configured. It replaces the
Screwing Operations accordion.
1. Click the Open Protocol Operations accordion.
2. Select an Open Protocol Operation and perform either of the following:
click

if the Operation has been completed, or if the Operator decides that it can be repaired

(Reason: Operator Backup). The Operation is automatically displayed in green and checked (
OK status).
click

if the Operation has not been executed, or if the Operator decides that it cannot be repaired

(Reason: Operation Failed). The Operation is automatically displayed in red and not checked (
NOK status).
click
if there is an Open Protocol Node compatible with the Operation to be repaired. In this
manner, the Rework Program Number is sent to the Open Protocol Node, thus starting the
communication and acquiring the related results.
3. (Optional) If necessary, click
in the Operation's tile to access a dedicated pane, containing details on the
Operation, such as values provided for rundown measurements.
4. (Optional) Repeat steps 2-3 for each Operation that must be amended.

### 13.11 Managing Line Side Positions

At runtime, the Operator can view and manage Line Side Positions and related Kanban Calls manually, performing
the following operations:
open a call. There must be no other active calls for the Line Side Position, and the difference between the actual
quantity and the maximum capacity of the Line Side Position must be at least of one Bin.
cancel a call (only if its state is Open or InProgress), for example if not required anymore or opened by mistake.
In addition, for Line Side Positions that are automatically managed, it is possible to:
update the quantity of the Materials associated with the Equipment.
increase the priority of an existing call, only if the call is active. Keep in mind that lower values indicate a higher
priority.
To allow you to interpret the Kanban request state at a glance, a dedicated color code has been adopted inside
each tile representing a Line Side Position. The following color combinations are possible:

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow

Background color

Color of Contained State

Shipped

White

Green

Open

White

Blue

InProgress

Light Blue

Blue

Delivered

Green

White

Cancelling

Orange

White

Cancelled

Red

White

Prerequisites
At least one Line Side Position has been configured and released.
To exchange Kanban Messages with the External System, the integration between Opcenter Execution Discrete
and Opcenter Connect MOM has been properly configured, as explained in section How to Integrate Opcenter
Execution Discrete with Opcenter Connect MOM of the Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation and Configuration
Manual.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Terminal, click
to access the Kanban Call Management page. The Materials associated
with the Equipment are displayed, with an indication of their Part Number and Line Side Position. Each tile
(representing a Material) displays the following information:
Parameter

Description

Current Quantity

Value indicating the Material current quantity.
In case of manual Line Side Positions, the Bins to be requested are displayed instead.

Method

Indicates if the Kanban call is Manual or Automatic.

Duration

Timestamp (expressed in minutes) indicating the Kanban call duration.

Priority

Number indicating the Kanban call priority.

2. If the state of the Kanban Call allows it, select a tile and, according to the action that must be performed, click
the buttons listed below (the state of the Kanban request changes accordingly):
to open a new call.
to cancel the call.
(visible only in case of automatically managed Line Side Positions)
priority.

to increase the Kanban call

How to Execute Production in the Operator Terminal for One Piece Flow
Managing Line Side Positions

(visible only in case of automatically managed Line Side Positions)

to update the Material quantity.

Managing Line Side Positions

## 14 How to Manage Inspection Orders

Inspection Orders can be used in production whenever there is the need to decouple quality inspection activities
from pure manufacturing activities.
To create Inspection Orders, it is necessary to define at least the following:
Quality Characteristics to be inspected. Any type is allowed (Attribute, Variable and Visual) as well as any
sampling Frequency (100%, Time Based, Part Based and Unit Based).
Inspection Definitions pointing to those Quality Characteristics
Materials/Equipment, with or without an MTU to be inspected
Inspection Operations using the Inspection Definition mentioned previously.
Optionally, you can create more complex Inspection Plans, composed by more Inspection Operations, to have a
complete plan of all the Inspections required on a production line and on a specific product. These plans are called
Inspection Master Plans.
Inspection Orders can be generated from Inspection Operations or Inspection Master Plans, depending on
production needs.
The complete set of functionalities related to Inspection Orders is provided by the Opcenter Execution Foundation
Work Instruction App.
For more information on all the procedures mentioned below to manage Inspection Orders, see section
How to Configure Quality Execution outside of Manufacturing Activities of the Opcenter Execution Foundation
User Manual.

Prerequisites
Quality Characteristics and Inspection Definitions are available in your system. For more information on how to
configure them, see section How to Manage Quality Characteristics and Inspection Definitions of the Opcenter
Execution Foundation User Manual.

Target User
Users with either the Production Coordinator or Product Engineer role can perform this operation.

Workflow
1. Click
Product and Production Configuration > Inspection Operations in the sidebar, to access the
Inspection Operations page. From this page, create an Inspection Operation.
2. Link an Inspection Definition to the Inspection Operation and enrich it with a Referenced Material and a piece of
Equipment.
3. (Optional) Click

Product and Production Configuration > Inspection Master Plans in the sidebar, to

access the
Inspection Master Plans page. From this page, create an Inspection Master Plan.
4. (Optional) Link one or more Inspection Operations to the Inspection Master Plan created previously.
5. Click
Production Coordination > Inspection Orders in the sidebar, to access the
page. From this page, create and release an Inspection Order.

Inspection Orders

6. Click
Shopfloor Execution > Inspection Operator Landing in the sidebar, to access the
Operator Landing page. From this page, execute the Inspection Order.

Inspection

While you execute Inspection Orders, Non-Conformances can be automatically created if the NonConformance on Defect or Failure configuration key is set to Failure.

Managing Line Side Positions

During the execution of a Quality Inspection, in case a Failure is detected, the Non-Conformance will be
created on the Material Tracking Unit only if the Material Tracking Unit Code has been specified inside the
page.
The format used for the natural identifier of this Quality Non-Conformance will contain a dedicated
timestamp indicating when it was automatically created.
Likewise, the Notes field of the Quality Non-Conformance will contain a reference to Characteristic
Representation Identifier.
If you intend to use Failures, but do not want Non-Conformances to be generated automatically whenever
a Failure is declared, you can simply remove event
handlers OnRuntimeAttributiveOrVariableFailureTraced and OnRuntimeVisualFailureTraced in
Solution Studio: for details, see section Removing Event Subscriptions on page Configuring Event
Subscriptions in the Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation and Configuration Manual.

Result
Once all the activities foreseen by the Inspection Order are completed, you can complete the Inspection Order from
the Inspection Orders page.

Managing Line Side Positions

## 15 How to Manage Non-Conformances

During the execution of manufacturing activities, shop floor operators might encounter anomalies in Work Order
Operations or in related entities. In such scenarios, operators can bring these anomalies to the attention of
Production Coordinators by declaring Non-Conformances. Operators can raise a Non-Conformance if:
A defect or failure was detected during production (Quality Non-Conformance).
They find that something needs to be changed in the production process (Change Non-Conformance, also
known as a Change Request).
During the execution of a Work Order Operation, an Operator can declare Non-Conformances both from
the Operator Landing page (legacy) and from the Operator Terminal for Low Code UI Apps.
In addition, the Production Coordinator can declare a Quality Non-Conformance:
on a Work Order Operation already completed (for example, if the Operator did not detect the anomaly during
production).
on any Machine, Material Tracking Unit or Tool, whenever deemed appropriate and not necessarily during
actual production execution.
If integration with the Teamcenter Share collaborative environment is enabled, it is possible to share documents
attached to Non-Conformances with other colleagues through Teamcenter Share.
Data Segregation Propagation
In the context of Data Segregation, if a Non-Conformance is created from Work Order Operation or
Material Tracking Unit contexts related to a segregated Work Order, the Data Segregation tag of the Work
Order is propagated also to the opened Non-Conformance and it will be visible in the Non-Conformances
page.
The Data Segregation tag is propagated also in case of Non-Conformances derived from scrapped Material
Tracking Units in the Operator Terminal for Complex Manufacturing or from quantities scrapped from
Work Order Operations or Steps in the Operator Terminal for Repetitive Manufacturing.
In case the produced Material Tracking Unit is related to different Work Orders having different Data
Segregation tags, if a Non-Conformance is opened for the Material Tracking Unit context, none of the Data
Segregation tags is propagated to the Non-Conformance.
If you are working from the Opcenter Execution Discrete Production Coordinator Low Code UI App, in
the Master page, you can filter data not only by Work Order Operation, but also by Equipment, Material
Tracking Unit Code, Material Tracking Unit Identifier, and Tool using the specific boxes on top of the page.
The information displayed in the various tabs is filtered accordingly.

Automatic Creation of Non-Conformances in Integration with Opcenter Connect
MOM
It is possible to automatically create Non-Conformances in integration with Opcenter Connect MOM. Quality NonConformances can be declared on:
Work Order Operations: if anomalies are detected during their execution. It is possible to automatically create
Quality Non-Conformances on Work Order Operations. For more information, see How to Automatically Create
Non-Conformances of type Quality on Work Order Operations in Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation and
Configuration Manual.
Machines: for example, if a Machine breaks down. It is possible to automatically create Quality NonConformances on Machines. For more information, see How to Automatically Create Non-Conformances of type
Quality on Machines in Opcenter Execution Discrete Installation and Configuration Manual.

Material Tracking Units: for example, if a component to be assembled is found defective. It is possible to
automatically create Quality Non-Conformances on Material Tracking Units. For more information, see How to
Automatically Create Non-Conformances of type Quality on Material Tracking Units in Opcenter Execution
Discrete Installation and Configuration Manual.
Tools: for example, if a Tool wears out before its predicted lifespan.

Target User
Users with the Operator and Product Coordinator role can perform this procedure.

Prerequisites
Depending on the requirement, the Non-Conformance on Defect or Failure configuration key value is set
to Defect or Failure.
The Failures or Sub-Failures for which the Non-Conformance is to be declared have been created from the
Failures page. Refer to How to Manage Failures and Quality Actions in the Opcenter Execution Foundation User
Manual for details.

Workflow
1. Declare a Non-Conformance:
(Operator): Quality or Change
(Production Coordinator): Quality on a Work Order Operation or Quality on a Machine, Material Tracking
Unit or Tool.
2. (Production Coordinator) Handle the Non-Conformance.
Quality
Change

### 15.1 Declaring Quality Non-Conformances during Production

If a Defect or Failure was detected during production, you must open a Quality Non-Conformance.

Prerequisite
The involved Work Order Operation is in status Open or Active.

Procedure
1. In the Operator Landing page, click Defects.
2. Depending on your configuration, do either of the following:
if you are using Defects, select the applicable Defect Group in the Defect Groups area.
if you are using Failures, select the required Failure in the Failures area.
3. If the selected item contains sub-groups or sub-failures, select the item of interest.
4. Select one or more Defect Types or Failures: the selections are displayed in the Selected Defect Types or the
Selected Failures area.
5. Click Add Defects/Add Failures.
6. In the Declare Non-Conformance panel, set the following parameters:

Parameters

Description

Context

The entity type on which the Non-Conformance is to be declared:
Work Order Operation
Machine
Material Tracking Unit
Tool

Identifier

Unique identifier to be assigned to the Non-Conformance. Once saved, this parameter can no
longer be modified.

Notes

A short description of the Defect observed.

Severity

The severity of the Defect. Possible values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

7. According to the value selected for the Context parameter, perform the required operations:
If Context is set to

Do the following

Work Order Operation

Set the following parameters:
Work Order: The Work Order to which the Non-Conformance is
associated. The field is pre-set with the current Work Order.
Work Order Operation: The Work Order Operation to which the NonConformance is associated. The field is pre-set with the current Work
Order Operation.
Set either of the following according to the production type:
Non-Conformance Quantity: (if the involved Work Order is of
type FullQuantity or TransferBatch) the number of instances
of the product for which the Non-Conformance is observed.
Note If the Non-Conformance is declared on a Container, the
quantity must be equal to the number of items contained in it.
Material Tracking Units area: (if the involved Work Order is of
type Serialized or FullSerialized) click Link and select the
Serial Numbers and/or Containers on which the NonConformance is to be declared.

Machine

In the Equipment area, click
Link, select one or more items on which the
Non-Conformance is to be declared and then click Link.

Material Tracking Unit

In the Material Tracking Units area, click
Link, select one or more items on
which the Non-Conformance is to be declared and then click Link.
This area shows both the individual MTUs and the Containers that may
be present. Declaring a Non-Conformance on a Container means
declaring the Non-Conformance on all the contained Material Tracking
Units.

If Context is set to

Do the following

Tool

In the Tools area, click
Link, select one or more items on which the NonConformance is to be declared and then click Link.

8. In the Documents area of the Declare Non-Conformance panel, click
Link. The list of available
Documents is displayed.
9. If the Document you want to add is not already in the list, click Import to import it.
10. Select one or more Documents from the list and click Link to associate them to the Non-Conformance.
If there are Segregation tags associated to the Document you want to link to a Non-Conformance, the
resulting association (Non-Conformance Attachment) will automatically inherit these tags.
11. Click Save.
12. (Optional) Operating in the Non-Conformances page or Non-Conformance Notifications page, open the
created Non-Conformance and switch to the Documents tab.
13. Select the document attached to the Non-Conformance and click
Send to Teamcenter Share, if you want
to share the documents with other users through the Teamcenter Share collaborative environment. The
documents will be uploaded to a project named as the related Work Order NId.

Results
A Non-Conformance is created in status Open.
The status of the last Work Order Operation making up the Work Order is changed to Future Hold. Its color is set
to red in the dependency graph.
If the proper signal has been configured, the notification bar displays a number (or increments the existing one)
on the

icon, thus prompting the Production Coordinator to manage the Non-Conformance.

### 15.2 Declaring Quality Non-Conformances on Work Order Operations

This procedure allows a Production Coordinator to declare a Quality Non-Conformance on Work Order Operations.
Contrary to an Operator, a Production Coordinator can declare Non-Conformances even on completed Work Order
Operations. For example, an anomaly not noticed by an Operator during production can be declared at a later time.
Based on the Configuration Key Allow Opening a Non-Conformance on Failure's Parent setting, NonConformance can be created on Failures and their respective Sub-Failures in the hierarchy.
While creating Non-Conformances, Failures can be filtered with respect to Material or Work Order
Operation based on the Configuration Key Runtime Failure Association Management setting.

Target User
Users with the Production Coordinator role can perform this procedure.

Workflow
1. Create the Non-Conformance.
2. Associate Defects or Failures to the Non-Conformance.
3. (optional) Associate Documents to the Non-Conformance

4. (optional) If one or more of the associated Documents are PNG or JPG pictures, highlight them.

Creating Non-Conformances
1. Click the

Non-Conformances tile in the home page

2. In the Non-Conformances page, click
Create.
3. In the Declare Non-Conformance page, set the following parameters:
Parameters

Description

Context

The entity type on which the Non-Conformance is to be declared. Set this parameter to
Work Order Operation.
Note Setting this parameter to other values allows you to declare Non-Conformances on
Production Entities.

Identifier

Unique identifier to be assigned to the Non-Conformance. Once saved, this parameter can
no longer be modified.

Notes

A short description of the defect observed.

Work Order

The Work Order to which the Non-Conformance is associated.

Work Order
Operation

The Work Order Operation to which the Non-Conformance is associated.
Note This field appears only after selecting a Work Order.

NonConformance
Quantity

The number of instances of the product for which the Non-Conformance is observed.
Note If the Non-Conformance is declared on a Container, the quantity must be equal to
the number of items contained in it.

Severity

The severity for the defect. Possible values: LOW, MEDIUM, HIGH.

4. If the Work Order is of Serialized or FullSerialized type, in the Material Tracking Unit area select the MTUs to
be linked to the Non-Conformance.
Note You can select both Material Tracking Units and Containers. Selecting a Container means selecting at the
same time all its contained MTUs.
5. To attach a supporting document such as a log file or a screenshot, in the Documents area either click
Import to import a document from the local filesystem or
Link to attach a document previously imported
into the system.
Note You can also associate Documents at a later time: see the Associating Documents to NonConformances paragraph for details.
6. Click Create.

Associating Defects or Failures to Non-Conformances
1. In the Non-Conformances page, select the Non-Conformance and click
2. Click the Defects tab.

Open.

3. Add previously-created Defects or Failures to the Non-Conformance by clicking
Defect Groups/Failures and Defect Types/Sub-Failures of interest.

Add and then selecting the

Associating Documents to Non-Conformances
If there are Segregation tags associated to the Document you want to link to a Non-Conformance, the
resulting association (Non-Conformance Attachment) will automatically inherit these tags.

2. Click the Documents tab.

Open.

3. Click
Link. The list of available Documents is displayed.
4. Do either of the following:
If the Document you want to add is not already in the list, click Import to import it.

5. (Optional) Select the document added in the previous steps and click
Send to Teamcenter Share, if you
want to share the documents with other users through the Teamcenter Share collaborative environment. The

Highlighting defects on pictures
If a document associated to a Non-Conformance is a picture in .JPG or .PNG format, you can draw colored lines or
shapes on it in order to highlight the defect.
1. In the Non-Conformances page, select the Non-Conformance and click
Open.
2. Click the Documents tab.
3. From the list of attached Documents, select a picture in .JPG or .PNG format.
4. Click
Red Lining.
5. In the Red Lining window, specify a meaningful Name to be used to differentiate the Document with the
highlights from the original Document.
6. Use the graphical instruments to draw lines, shapes or to type short notes on the picture. If required, you can
also use the

Eraser icon to edit and reapply the highlighting.

If you are using the system localized in Japanese and you want to type text by using the IME PAD
interface, set the focus on the text box, right-click on IME Options in the Taskbar and select IME Pad.
7. Click Save. A new picture containing the highlights is created and associated to the Non-Conformance: the
indicator will be displayed inside the tile representing the new Document to specify that the .JPG or .PNG
file contained therein has been red-lined.

Result
A Non-Conformance is created in status Open.
If the proper signal has been configured, the notification bar displays a number (or increments the existing one)
on the

icon thus prompting the Production Coordinator to manage the Non-Conformance.
